/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# PLAN: Turnkey C# SDK ↔ 公式実装 同等性検証プラン (v3)
     2	
     3	**Status**: Draft v3 — awaiting Codex plan review (target: review-clean).
     4	
     5	## v3 changes vs v2 (Codex r1 後の修正)
     6	
     7	| Codex r1 指摘 | v3 の修正 |
     8	|---|---|
     9	| `tests/Fixtures/*.json` の link が壊れている (B-行 broken link) | 全箇所を `tests/Fixtures/{encoding,crypto,api-key-stamper}/*.json` に修正 |
    10	| **HPKE/QOS 混同 (致命)**: v2 は「Turnkey HPKE は RFC 9180 でない独自構成」と書いたが、実は SDK `hpkeEncrypt/hpkeDecrypt` ([crypto.ts:88-138](../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/ts-source/crypto.ts)) は **JSdoc で RFC 9180 を明示**しており構成も HPKE 風。QOS `quorumKeyEncrypt` ([crypto.ts:219+](../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/ts-source/crypto.ts)) は別物で C# **未ポート** | **canonical wording を Section 2 に統一**: (a) C# が port しているのは SDK HPKE (RFC 9180 を意図) のみ、(b) QOS は scope 外、(c) SDK HPKE が RFC 9180 と byte-equal かは別 PR で評価する step を新設 (PR-7a)。Tier-3 RFC 9180 取り込みは **PR-7a の評価結果次第** で確定する条件付き |
    11	| Tier-2 の決定論性が崩れる: SDK HPKE は ephemeral key 生成、QOS も nonce 生成 → encrypt 出力 bytes は毎回違う | **encrypt の byte snapshot は撤回**。代わりに (a) **SDK 生成済みの round-trip 等価** (上流 Node で encrypt した output を **fixture として固定**しておき、C# で **decrypt して plaintext 等価**を確認)、(b) encrypt 出力構造 (lengths, header bytes) の構造的 assertion、(c) HPKE が ephemeral key を **外部 inject できる private API** が無いか追加調査 (調査結果次第で encrypt の byte snapshot を限定的に復活) |
    12	| @noble/curves v1.3.0 low-S deterministic が load-bearing だが pinned snapshot だけでは証明できない | PR-4 で **transitive deps を exact lock** し、**generator 起動時に既知 vector で `purejs.sign(message, privateKey)` を assert** する pre-flight test を追加。失敗したら fixture 生成中断 |
    13	| monthly drift workflow が unbounded で月毎に issue 量産 | **idempotency rule** を Section 6.4 に追加: `gh issue list --label upstream-drift --json number --jq '.[0].number'` で既存 open issue を 1 つ取得、あれば update、無ければ create。Workflow yaml 例も入れる |
    14	| PR-4 が `tests/turnkey-sdk-csharp.Tests.csproj` の Generators 排除を含んでいない | PR-4 内容に「`<None Remove="Fixtures/Generators/**" />` と `<Content Remove="Fixtures/Generators/**" />` を csproj に追加」を明記 |
    15	| PR-3 規模が plan の表現より大きい | Section 6.1 に **欠落候補テスト名の concrete list** を追加 (Codex r1 列挙分を全部書き出す) |
    16	| Tier-3 で Ed25519 / secp256k1 / Base58Check Bitcoin を忘れている | Section 6.3 に Ed25519 RFC 8032、secp256k1 SEC2、Base58Check Bitcoin double-SHA256 を追加 |
    17	| PR-11 fixture-only seeding は consumer 無し golden data | PR-11 で **`ProofFixtureProvenanceTests` を必ず一緒に追加**: JSON parse、`_provenance` schema 検証、上流 sha256 一致、を assert。verify 実装本体は scope 外を維持 |
    18	| Phase E は `PR-12 (docs)` 誤記 (PR-12 は drift CI) | `PR-14 (docs)` に修正、PR-14 の依存に PR-12/PR-13 を追加 |
    19	| HPKE 説明が Section 2/6.3/10/12 で重複 | Section 2 を canonical source とし、他 3 箇所は `(Section 2 参照)` リンクに置換 |
    20	| "README CI で更新" / "backend accepts means correct" が測定不能 | "README updated and committed in PR-14" / "wire-format byte-equal in Tier-2 fixtures" に置換 |
    21	| 「公式他言語 SDK の test fixture」未検証 (G の unresolved) | Phase A の作業に **`peak/` リポジトリの grep で proof 呼出し有無を確認**する step を明示。他言語 SDK fixture は本プラン scope 外として明記 |
    22	
    23	## v2 changes vs v1 (調査後の修正、参考)
    24	
    25	| v1 の誤り | v2 の修正 |
    26	|---|---|
    27	| 「Tier 3 で HPKE RFC 9180 vectors を取り込む」 | (v3 で再修正) v2 では「Turnkey HPKE は RFC 9180 でない独自方式」とした上で RFC 9180 vectors を撤回したが、これも誤り。v3 で再評価 |
    28	| proof.ts は scope 外で確定 | proof-tests.ts に**実 enclave 由来の `v1AppProof` / `v1BootProof` JSON ゴールデン**があり、cross-language 借用に最適。**proof ポート要否は別計画**を維持しつつ、本プランで `tests/Fixtures/proofs/` を Tier-1 として置く準備 (verify 実装は別計画) を含める |
    29	| 「他言語 SDK に cross-language vector があるかもしれない」 | 公式 cross-language fixture セットは**存在しない**。代わりに `mockSenderPrivateKey=67ee05fc…` 等のハードコード値が `tkhq/sdk` / `swift-sdk` / `dart-sdk` で**完全一致でコピー**。実質的 reference vector として **Tier-1 で借用** |
    30	| Tier-4 (live) | 維持。live e2e は `tkhq/python-sdk` のみが CI に組み込んでおり、他 SDK は mock 中心 |
    31	
    32	## 1. Intent
    33	
    34	`KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk` が **Turnkey 公式 TypeScript SDK
    35	(@turnkey/{crypto,http,api-key-stamper,encoding} の peak-pinned 版)
    36	と機能的に等価** であることを、後付け的なレビューではなく
    37	**機械検証可能な公式由来テストアセット** で恒常的に担保する。
    38	
    39	本プランは、既存の Codex 3-round 行ごと等価レビュー
    40	(`codex-crypto-reviews/`) と既存の Tier-1 fixture
    41	(`tests/Fixtures/{encoding,crypto,api-key-stamper}/*.json`) を
    42	**置き換えるものではない**。
    43	それらが「**人が読んで一致を確認した**」レベルの保証であるのに対し、
    44	本プランは「**入力に対する観測可能な出力を機械で照合し、
    45	上流が変わったら自動で気付く**」レベルの保証を上乗せする。
    46	
    47	「観測可能な出力」は具体的には以下のいずれか:
    48	
    49	- 決定論経路: 入力が同じなら byte 等価 (例: stamp の DER 署名は
    50	  `purejs` runtime で RFC 6979 deterministic-k、credential-bundle decrypt は
    51	  入力 ciphertext + receiver private key で plaintext が一意)。
    52	- 非決定論経路: ephemeral key/nonce を生成するため byte 等価は取れない
    53	  ものに対しては (a) decrypt round-trip での plaintext 等価、
    54	  (b) 構造的 assertion (フィールド長、ヘッダ、JSON キー順、署名検証成功)。
    55	
    56	## 2. Goals (must-have)
    57	
    58	1. **Coverage matrix**: 公式 `__tests__/*.ts` の各 `test()` ブロックが
    59	   C# のどの `[Fact]` / `[Theory]` メソッドに対応するかを 1:1 で
    60	   ドキュメント化する。欠落は CI で fail する。
    61	2. **Byte-equal fixtures**: 公式 npm パッケージを `npm install` した
    62	   Node 環境で生成した実バイト (stamp, signed-request body,
    63	   credential-bundle decrypt 等) を `tests/Fixtures/**` に固定し、
    64	   C# が同一入力で同一バイトを生成することを assert する。
    65	3. **Standard test vectors (primitives + 条件付きで HPKE)**:
    66	   HKDF (RFC 5869)、ECDSA P-256 deterministic-k (RFC 6979)、
    67	   AES-GCM-256 (NIST CAVP)、SHA-256 (NIST SHS)、HMAC-SHA256 (RFC 4231)、
    68	   Ed25519 (RFC 8032、SOLANA export 用) の **個別 primitive** で C# 実装を検証する。
    69	   secp256k1 SEC2 / Base58Check Bitcoin double-SHA256 もここに含める。
    70	   **HPKE 全体 (RFC 9180) の取り込みは条件付き**:
    71	   PR-7a で 「pinned `@turnkey/crypto@2.8.8` の `hpkeEncrypt` が
    72	   RFC 9180 base mode (DHKEM(P-256,HKDF-SHA256) + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM)
    73	   と byte-equal か」を `__tests__/crypto-test.ts` の HPKE 経路を
    74	   実 Node で動かして固定 ephemeral key を inject (もし可能なら) し評価する。
    75	   評価結果が **byte-equal なら RFC 9180 Appendix A vectors を取り込み**、
    76	   **byte-equal でなければ取り込まない** (Tier-2 で SDK 生成 bytes を信頼)。
    77	   ※ JSdoc は RFC 9180 を明示するが ([crypto.ts:88-91](../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/ts-source/crypto.ts))、
    78	   実装の suite_id/labels と RFC 9180 base mode の literal が**完全一致するかは未確認**。
    79	   QOS `quorumKeyEncrypt` は C# **未ポート**であり本プラン scope 外
    80	   (Section 2 canonical 説明を参照)。
    81	4. **Drift detection**: 公式 `__tests__/` の sha256 を pin し、
    82	   月次 cron で再取得して差分があれば **upstream-drift というラベルの
    83	   single open issue を idempotent に update/create する**
    84	   (Section 6.4 / Section 8 / Section 9 で一貫した定義)。
    85	   この workflow は **main CI gate ではない** (= PR をブロックしない)。
    86	   issue が立ったら人手で再 pin と対応 C# テスト更新の PR を立てる。
    87	5. **不確定要素の明示** (PR-14 README "Verification posture" 章で
    88	   以下のチェックリストを必ず文書化する):
    89	   - [ ] 公式 P-256 ECDSA は `nodecrypto.ts` / `webcrypto.ts` ランタイムでは
    90	     non-deterministic。C# 側は **`purejs` ランタイム相当 (RFC 6979 + low-S) のみ
    91	     ports** している事実
    92	     (既に `ApiKeyStamper.cs-codex-findings-reconciliation.md` E1/E2 で合意済み)。
    93	   - [ ] SDK HPKE と RFC 9180 base mode の byte-equality は **PR-7a の判定結果**を
    94	     review posture に明記 (byte-equal / non-equal / inconclusive)。
    95	   - [ ] **未ポート API リスト** (`src/Crypto.cs:25-30` の "Out of scope" コメントと一致):
    96	     `hpkeAuthEncrypt`, `quorumKeyEncrypt`, `extractPrivateKeyFromPKCS8Bytes`,
    97	     `fromDerSignature`, `toDerSignature`, `verifyStampSignature`,
    98	     `verifyRequestStamp`, `encryptWalletToBundle`, `encryptToEnclave`,
    99	     `encryptOauth2ClientSecret`, `encryptOnRampSecret`, `proof.ts`。
   100	   - [ ] live e2e backend 経路は opt-in (`TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_API_KEY` 環境変数) のみ。
   101	   - [ ] cross-language 借用値 (`mockSenderPrivateKey`, `mockCredentialBundle`, etc.)
   102	     の上流 git revision pin。
   103	
   104	### Non-goals
   105	
   106	- 既存の `codex-crypto-reviews/` SOP の置き換え (補強のみ)。
   107	- nuget.org への公開条件の変更。
   108	- 新規機能 (proof.ts 完全移植、QOS `quorumKeyEncrypt` ポートなど) の追加。
   109	  **proof モジュールに対するポート方針は本プランの第 12 章「未スコープ依存」で別計画とする。**
   110	- 公式 Backend に対する live e2e の必須化 (live 経路は opt-in のまま)。
   111	
   112	### 2.1. 暗号構成の canonical 用語 (重要)
   113	
   114	以下の用語は本プラン全体で **canonical** にこの定義で使う。他章 (3, 4, 6.3, 9, 10, 12) は
   115	**この章を再記述せず参照する**。
   116	
   117	- **SDK HPKE**: pinned `@turnkey/crypto@2.8.8` の `hpkeEncrypt` / `hpkeDecrypt` /
   118	  `hpkeAuthEncrypt` 経路。([crypto.ts:88-203](../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/ts-source/crypto.ts))
   119	  - JSdoc が RFC 9180 を明示。
   120	  - `SUITE_ID_1 = "KEM\0\x10"` (DHKEM(P-256, HKDF-SHA256))、
   121	    `SUITE_ID_2 = "HPKE\0\x10\0\x01\0\x02"` (kem_id=0x0010, kdf_id=0x0001, aead_id=0x0002)
   122	    の literal を使う ([Crypto.cs:77-79](../src/Crypto.cs))。
   123	  - これらの suite_id literal は **RFC 9180 base mode の値と一致**:
   124	    `kem_id=0x0010 = DHKEM(P-256, HKDF-SHA256)`,
   125	    `kdf_id=0x0001 = HKDF-SHA256`, `aead_id=0x0002 = AES-256-GCM`。
   126	    `extractAndExpand(..., 32)` で 32-byte AES key、`extractAndExpand(..., 12)` で
   127	    12-byte IV を派生するのも AES-256-GCM の使用と整合。
   128	  - ただし上記 suite_id 一致は「実装が RFC 9180 と完全互換」を即座に意味しない。
   129	    HPKE label の literal バイト列 (`HPKE-v1`, `eae_prk`, `secret`, `shared_secret`)
   130	    も RFC 9180 と完全一致するかは PR-7a で確認する。
   131	  - **C# `Turnkey.Crypto.HpkeEncrypt` / `HpkeDecrypt` がポートしているのはこの経路のみ**。
   132	    ([Crypto.cs:404-456](../src/Crypto.cs))。
   133	- **QOS encryption**: 同じファイルの `quorumKeyEncrypt` ([crypto.ts:219+](../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/ts-source/crypto.ts))。
   134	  - jsdoc に「Algorithm originally implemented in qos」と明示。
   135	  - `QOS_ENCRYPTION_HMAC_MESSAGE = "qos_encryption_hmac_message"`、
   136	    SHA-512 HMAC + AES-GCM-256、borsh serialization。
   137	  - **C# **未ポート**。本プラン scope 外**。
   138	- **RFC 9180**: IETF Hybrid Public Key Encryption 仕様。SDK HPKE と完全一致するかは
   139	  PR-7a で評価する別タスク。「SDK HPKE = RFC 9180 と等価」とは現時点で言わない。
   140	
   141	### 2.2. C# が ports している upstream 経路の一覧 (Non-Goal 確認用)
   142	
   143	| C# public API | upstream 関数 | upstream 経路 | C# port 状況 |
   144	|---|---|---|---|
   145	| `Turnkey.Crypto.HpkeEncrypt` | `hpkeEncrypt` | SDK HPKE (RFC 9180 を意図) | **済** |
   146	| `Turnkey.Crypto.HpkeDecrypt` | `hpkeDecrypt` | SDK HPKE | **済** |
   147	| `Turnkey.Crypto.DecryptCredentialBundle` | `decryptCredentialBundle` | SDK HPKE + base58check + AES-GCM | **済** |
   148	| `Turnkey.Crypto.DecryptExportBundle` | `decryptExportBundle` | SDK HPKE + mnemonic/PKCS#8 | **済** |
   149	| `Turnkey.Crypto.EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle` | `encryptPrivateKeyToBundle` | SDK HPKE + base58check | **済** |
   150	| (なし) | `quorumKeyEncrypt` (QOS) | QOS 独自 | **未ポート (scope 外)** |
   151	| (なし) | `verify` / `verifyAppProofSignature` (proof) | P-256/P-384 + COSE/X.509 | **未ポート (proof scope 外)** |
   152	
   153	## 3. 現状アセスメント (Where we are)
   154	
   155	| 項目 | 既存状態 | 備考 |
   156	|---|---|---|
   157	| Upstream pinned snapshots | 4 パッケージ (`crypto@2.8.8`, `http@3.16.0`, `api-key-stamper@0.5.0`, `encoding@0.6.0`) を `codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/` に **extracted npm tarball contents + 記録された tarball sha256** で固定 (`.tgz` ファイルは worktree にコミットしない方針、`tarball-checksums.txt` で sha256 のみ pin) | [turnkey-source-pins.md](../codex-crypto-reviews/turnkey-source-pins.md) |
   158	| Tier-1 hand-transcribed fixtures | `tests/Fixtures/{encoding,crypto,api-key-stamper}/turnkey-*-vectors.json` の 3 ファイル。`_provenance` メタデータと `_source_line` 付き | `tests/Fixtures/http/` は**未整備** |
   159	| 3-round Codex review | 6 ファイル全部 review-clean、`FINAL-INTEGRATED-REVIEW-20260523.md` で v0.1.0-alpha GO 済み | [FINAL-INTEGRATED-REVIEW-20260523.md](../codex-crypto-reviews/FINAL-INTEGRATED-REVIEW-20260523.md) |
   160	| C# test 数 (合計約 113 `[Fact]/[Theory]`) | Encoding 48 / Crypto ~45 / Stamper 10 / Http 11 | 公式 (~30 `test()`) より細かいが、対応マッピングは未文書化 |
   161	| Drift 検知 | `tarball-checksums.txt` のみ。`__tests__/*.ts` 自体の sha256 pin は無し | 公式テスト更新時に気付かないリスク |
   162	| Node 生成 byte fixture | **なし** (`tests/Fixtures/Generators/` 未作成) | `Tier-1 README.md` に「node-generated 予定」と明記済み |
   163	| RFC / NIST vectors | HKDF RFC 5869 A1-A3 のみ (`tests/CryptoTests.cs:91/111/149`) | HPKE 9180、ECDSA 6979 単独、AES-GCM CAVP は未取り込み |
   164	
   165	### 公式テストの実状 (2026-05-27 時点・調査済み)
   166	
   167	公式 `__tests__/*.ts` は **`__fixtures__/api-key.{private,public,public.pem}`**
   168	というローカルファイルを `readFixture()` で読む構造で、
   169	他言語に直接持ち込める JSON 形式の test vector dataset は**公開されていない**。
   170	npm tarball にはテストもフィクスチャも含まれないため、
   171	**GitHub の `tkhq/sdk` リポジトリの該当タグからコピーする**のが唯一の入口。
   172	これは既存の `upstream-snapshots/.../ts-source/__fixtures__/` で対応済み。
   173	
   174	公式テストの assertion 強度は (1) DER ECDSA 検証 + (2) スキーム文字列 +
   175	(3) JSON キー順 + (4) publicKey 等価 のみ。**バイト等価 snapshot は無い**
   176	(P-256 `nodecrypto.ts` は random-k なため bytes が毎回変わる)。
   177	したがって C# 側で「`purejs` 等価のバイト」を追加で検証することは、
   178	**公式テストより厳しい superset の検証**になる (=価値が高い)。
   179	
   180	#### 公式他言語 SDK の検証スタイル (調査結果)
   181	
   182	- **cross-language fixture セットは存在しない**。
   183	  代わりに `mockSenderPrivateKey="67ee05fc..."`、
   184	  `mockPrivateKey="20fa65df..."`、`mockCredentialBundle="w99a5xV6..."`、
   185	  ニーモニック `"leaf lady until indicate praise final route toast cake minimum insect unknown"`、
   186	  PEM 鍵 `487f361d…0213c` / 圧縮公開鍵 `02f739f8…0316` が
   187	  **`tkhq/sdk` / `swift-sdk` / `dart-sdk` の 3 レポで完全一致のコピー**で
   188	  共有されており、実質的な reference vector になっている。
   189	  → C# でも同じ hex / mnemonic を使えば、3 言語と機械的に三角検証できる。
   190	- **`tkhq/sdk/packages/crypto/src/__tests__/proof-tests.ts`** には
   191	  **実 enclave が発行した複数の `v1AppProof` / `v1BootProof` JSON**
   192	  がフルで埋め込み済。署名検証ロジックの cross-language ゴールデン
   193	  として現状最高品質。
   194	- **`tkhq/go-sdk/pkg/enclave_encrypt/encrypt_test.go::TestP256Verify`**
   195	  は `// Values generated by rust code` コメント付きの **Go↔Rust の
   196	  cross-language ECDSA vector**。「ある言語の出力を別言語の入力に使う」
   197	  という稀有な明示例。
   198	- **`tkhq/qos/src/qos_p256/SPEC.md`** は **QOS encryption の独自仕様書**
   199	  (SDK HPKE とは別経路。Section 2.1 参照)。
   200	  ECDH(P-256) → HMAC-SHA512 で AES-GCM-256 の key を派生、
   201	  `QOS_ENCRYPTION_HMAC_MESSAGE` 定数、nonce 12B など、
   202	  **RFC 9180 とは互換性のない独自構成**。
   203	  C# は QOS をポートしておらず本プラン scope 外。
   204	- 公式 backend に対する live e2e を CI に組み込んでいるのは
   205	  `tkhq/python-sdk` のみ。他はすべて mocked。
   206	
   207	## 4. 既存の検証ギャップ (Why this plan)
   208	
   209	GAP-1. **Coverage matrix 不在** — 公式 `test("...")` と C# `[Fact]` Method の
   210	       対応表が無く、公式が test を追加したときに C# 側で漏れる。
   211	GAP-2. **HTTP fixture 不在** — `tests/Fixtures/http/turnkey-http-vectors.json`
   212	       が無い ([Http.cs-codex-findings-reconciliation.md セクション F](../codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-codex-findings-reconciliation.md))。
   213	GAP-3. **Node 生成バイト fixture 不在** — `Generators/` 機構が無いので
   214	       `purejs` モードで実 Node が吐く DER 署名や `SignedRequest.body`
   215	       バイトと C# 出力を比較できない。
   216	GAP-4. **Drift 検知 (テスト側) 不在** — `__tests__/*.ts` 自体の sha256 が
   217	       pin されていない。上流テスト更新を catch できない。
   218	GAP-5. **Primitive 単独ベクトル不足** — HKDF 以外 (ECDSA P-256 RFC 6979 単独・
   219	       AES-GCM-256 NIST CAVP・HMAC-SHA256 RFC 4231・SHA-256 NIST SHS・
   220	       Ed25519 RFC 8032・secp256k1 SEC2・Base58Check Bitcoin double-SHA256) の
   221	       単体テストが無い。SDK HPKE 全体 (RFC 9180 互換性は未確認、Section 2.1 参照)
   222	       の取り込みは PR-7a の評価結果次第とし、それまでは primitives 単独ベクトル
   223	       でカバーする。
   224	GAP-6. **proof モジュール非対応** — `crypto/proof.ts` (129 行) と
   225	       `proof-tests.ts` (実 enclave 由来の JSON ゴールデン) が C# に
   226	       存在しない。**proof のポート要否は別計画**だが、本プランでは
   227	       「ゴールデン JSON を Fixture 側に取り込む準備」 (実コード非実装) は
   228	       含める。proof 検証は ECDSA P-256 + base64url 復号で実現できるため、
   229	       既存 C# プリミティブで verify ロジックだけ書ける見込みが立つかは
   230	       別プランで判定する。
   231	GAP-7. **他言語 SDK との同名 hex 値共有が活用されていない** —
   232	       `mockSenderPrivateKey` 等の同名定数が 3 言語で完全一致。
   233	       これを C# 側でも採用すれば三角検証ができるが、現状ローカルで
   234	       別の値を生成しているテストがある (例: `Hpke_EncryptThenDecrypt_RoundTripsArbitraryPayload`)。
   235	
   236	## 5. 検証戦略 (4 Tier)
   237	
   238	```
   239	Tier 1: Upstream-transcribed JSON vectors    [PARTIAL — encoding/crypto/stamper のみ]
   240	        ↑ どこから来た値か Provenance 必須
   241	Tier 2: Node-generated byte snapshots        [NEW]
   242	        ↑ 公式 npm を実 Node で動かして固定。`purejs` ランタイム選択
   243	Tier 3: Standard RFC / NIST vectors          [NEW (HKDF を除き)]
   244	        ↑ 上流が間違うリスクの保険
   245	Tier 4: Live backend (opt-in)                [既存スキップ条件付き]
   246	        ↑ env 変数があるときのみ
   247	```
   248	
   249	各 Tier の **provenance** を `tests/Fixtures/README.md` のレベル分類
   250	(`upstream-test-vectors` / `node-generated` / `rfc` / `nist` /
   251	`turnkey-sample`) で必ず明示する。
   252	
   253	## 6. 設計 — コンポーネント分解
   254	
   255	### 6.1 Coverage matrix generator (新規)
   256	
   257	**目的**: 公式 `__tests__/*.ts` の `test()/it()` ブロック名一覧と、
   258	C# テストの `[Fact]` メソッド名一覧をクロス参照し、対応表を生成する。
   259	
   260	**実装案**: TypeScript ではなく **POSIX `awk` + dotnet test 一覧** のシェル

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba codex-crypto-reviews/coverage-map.sh | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	#
     3	# coverage-map.sh — generate / verify the upstream test → C# test coverage matrix.
     4	#
     5	# Modes:
     6	#   coverage-map.sh           — write coverage-map.tsv + coverage-map.md (no gate)
     7	#   coverage-map.sh --check   — write outputs AND exit non-zero if any
     8	#                                upstream test is MISSING from C# coverage and
     9	#                                has no N/A reason recorded in coverage-map.na.tsv.
    10	#
    11	# Inputs:
    12	#   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/*/ts-source/__tests__/*-test*.ts
    13	#                                 — upstream test files (extracted npm tarball,
    14	#                                   not the test() blocks inside compiled output)
    15	#   tests/*Tests.cs              — C# test files. Each test method's coverage is
    16	#                                  declared by an XML doc-comment of the form:
    17	#                                    /// upstream: <relpath>:<line> "<test name>"
    18	#                                  placed immediately above the [Fact]/[Theory]
    19	#                                  attribute. The annotation is hand-maintained.
    20	#   codex-crypto-reviews/coverage-map.na.tsv
    21	#                                 — manually-curated rows for upstream tests that
    22	#                                  the C# SDK deliberately does NOT port. Each
    23	#                                  row is TAB-separated:
    24	#                                    <upstream_relpath>:<line><TAB><reason>
    25	#                                  The reason MUST be non-empty (the gate rejects
    26	#                                  empty reasons).
    27	#
    28	# Outputs:
    29	#   codex-crypto-reviews/coverage-map.tsv
    30	#                                 — joined matrix, one row per upstream test.
    31	#   codex-crypto-reviews/coverage-map.md
    32	#                                 — same data rendered for human review.
    33	
    34	set -euo pipefail
    35	
    36	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
    37	REVIEWS_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/codex-crypto-reviews"
    38	UPSTREAM_DIR="$REVIEWS_DIR/upstream-snapshots"
    39	TESTS_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/tests"
    40	NA_FILE="$REVIEWS_DIR/coverage-map.na.tsv"
    41	TSV_OUT="$REVIEWS_DIR/coverage-map.tsv"
    42	MD_OUT="$REVIEWS_DIR/coverage-map.md"
    43	
    44	CHECK_MODE=0
    45	if [[ "${1:-}" == "--check" ]]; then
    46	  CHECK_MODE=1
    47	fi
    48	
    49	cd "$REPO_ROOT"
    50	
    51	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
    52	# Step 1. Enumerate upstream tests.
    53	#
    54	# Format emitted to TMP_UPSTREAM (TAB-separated):
    55	#   <relpath>:<line>\t<test-name>
    56	#
    57	# Handles two source forms:
    58	#   (a) test("name", ...)            — single-string test()/it()
    59	#   (b) test.each([...])("name", …)  — parametrized; name template can contain $var
    60	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
    61	
    62	TMP_UPSTREAM="$(mktemp)"
    63	trap 'rm -f "$TMP_UPSTREAM" "$TMP_MAPPED" "$TMP_NA" "$TMP_REPORT" 2>/dev/null || true' EXIT
    64	TMP_MAPPED="$(mktemp)"
    65	TMP_NA="$(mktemp)"
    66	TMP_REPORT="$(mktemp)"
    67	
    68	# Use find -print0 + xargs is safer for spaces but our path tree has no spaces.
    69	# Keep simple: glob expansion with sorted ordering.
    70	UPSTREAM_FILES=()
    71	while IFS= read -r f; do
    72	  UPSTREAM_FILES+=("$f")
    73	done < <(find "$UPSTREAM_DIR" -type f \
    74	            \( -name '*-test.ts' -o -name '*-tests.ts' \) \
    75	            -path '*/__tests__/*' | sort)
    76	
    77	for f in "${UPSTREAM_FILES[@]}"; do
    78	  rel="${f#$REPO_ROOT/}"
    79	  awk -v rel="$rel" '
    80	    BEGIN { in_each=0; each_line=0 }
    81	    # match "test(" / "it(" with a literal-string first argument
    82	    /^[[:space:]]*(test|it)[[:space:]]*\(["'"'"']/ {
    83	      line=NR
    84	      # extract quoted name. allow either " or '"'"'
    85	      s=$0
    86	      sub(/^[[:space:]]*(test|it)[[:space:]]*\(["'"'"']/, "", s)
    87	      # cut at trailing matching quote — heuristic: last "," is the arg sep
    88	      # so split at the last ", " preceding "(" / "=>"
    89	      # simpler: cut at first unescaped trailing quote followed by comma
    90	      n=index(s, "\","); if (n==0) n=index(s, "'"'"',")
    91	      if (n>0) {
    92	        name=substr(s, 1, n-1)
    93	      } else {
    94	        # fallback: strip leading quote, take until next quote
    95	        name=s
    96	        sub(/["'"'"'].*$/, "", name)
    97	      }
    98	      printf("%s:%d\t%s\n", rel, line, name)
    99	      next
   100	    }
   101	    # match "test.each([" / "it.each([" — record the line; the template comes
   102	    # on a later line and looks like:  ])("template", …)
   103	    /^[[:space:]]*(test|it)\.each[[:space:]]*\([[:space:]]*\[/ {
   104	      in_each=1; each_line=NR; next
   105	    }
   106	    in_each && /^[[:space:]]*\]\)[[:space:]]*\(["'"'"']/ {
   107	      s=$0
   108	      sub(/^[[:space:]]*\]\)[[:space:]]*\(["'"'"']/, "", s)
   109	      n=index(s, "\","); if (n==0) n=index(s, "'"'"',")
   110	      if (n>0) name=substr(s,1,n-1); else { name=s; sub(/["'"'"'].*$/, "", name) }
   111	      printf("%s:%d\t%s\n", rel, each_line, name)
   112	      in_each=0
   113	      next
   114	    }
   115	  ' "$f"
   116	done > "$TMP_UPSTREAM"
   117	
   118	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
   119	# Step 2. Enumerate C# test annotations.
   120	#
   121	# Find every line of form:
   122	#   /// upstream: <relpath>:<line> "<name>"
   123	# and pair it with the closest following [Fact]/[Theory] + method.
   124	#
   125	# Output format (TAB-separated):
   126	#   <relpath>:<line>\t<cs-file>:<cs-line>\t<cs-method>
   127	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
   128	
   129	for f in "$TESTS_DIR"/*Tests.cs; do
   130	  [ -f "$f" ] || continue
   131	  rel="${f#$REPO_ROOT/}"
   132	  awk -v csfile="$rel" '
   133	    function reset_pending() { delete pending; pending_n=0 }
   134	    BEGIN { pending_n=0 }
   135	    /^[[:space:]]*\/\/\/[[:space:]]*upstream:[[:space:]]/ {
   136	      # parse: /// upstream: <key> [..."<name>"]
   137	      # capture the first space-delimited token after "upstream:"
   138	      s=$0
   139	      sub(/^[[:space:]]*\/\/\/[[:space:]]*upstream:[[:space:]]*/, "", s)
   140	      # split by whitespace, take first field as the key
   141	      n=split(s, parts, /[[:space:]]+/)
   142	      key=parts[1]
   143	      pending_n++
   144	      pending[pending_n]=key
   145	      next
   146	    }
   147	    /^[[:space:]]*public[[:space:]]+(async[[:space:]]+)?(void|Task)[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*[[:space:]]*\(/ {
   148	      if (pending_n > 0) {
   149	        s=$0
   150	        sub(/^[[:space:]]*public[[:space:]]+(async[[:space:]]+)?(void|Task)[[:space:]]+/, "", s)
   151	        sub(/[[:space:]]*\(.*$/, "", s)
   152	        for (i=1; i<=pending_n; i++) {
   153	          printf("%s\t%s:%d\t%s\n", pending[i], csfile, NR, s)
   154	        }
   155	        reset_pending()
   156	      }
   157	      next
   158	    }
   159	    # If we see a non-comment non-attribute line before the public decl,
   160	    # drop any pending annotations (defensive: prevents drift if a /// upstream
   161	    # comment sits orphaned at the end of a region without a matching method).
   162	    /^[[:space:]]*\}/ && pending_n > 0 { reset_pending() }
   163	  ' "$f"
   164	done > "$TMP_MAPPED"
   165	
   166	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
   167	# Step 3. Load N/A entries.
   168	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
   169	
   170	if [ -f "$NA_FILE" ]; then
   171	  awk -F '\t' '
   172	    /^[[:space:]]*#/ { next }
   173	    NF == 0 { next }
   174	    {
   175	      key=$1
   176	      reason=$2
   177	      printf("%s\t%s\n", key, reason)
   178	    }
   179	  ' "$NA_FILE" > "$TMP_NA"
   180	else
   181	  : > "$TMP_NA"
   182	fi
   183	
   184	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
   185	# Step 4. Join — emit one row per upstream test.
   186	#
   187	# TSV columns (header on row 1):
   188	#   upstream_key  upstream_name  status  csharp_file  csharp_line  csharp_method  reason
   189	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
   190	
   191	{
   192	  printf "upstream_key\tupstream_name\tstatus\tcsharp_target\tcsharp_method\treason\n"
   193	  awk -F '\t' \
   194	      -v mapped="$TMP_MAPPED" \
   195	      -v na="$TMP_NA" '
   196	    function load_mapped() {
   197	      while ((getline line < mapped) > 0) {
   198	        n=split(line, p, "\t")
   199	        if (n >= 3) {
   200	          k=p[1]
   201	          # store comma-separated list of cs targets
   202	          if (k in mapk) {
   203	            mapk[k] = mapk[k] ", " p[2] " " p[3]
   204	          } else {
   205	            mapk[k] = p[2] " " p[3]
   206	          }
   207	        }
   208	      }
   209	      close(mapped)
   210	    }
   211	    function load_na() {
   212	      while ((getline line < na) > 0) {
   213	        n=split(line, p, "\t")
   214	        if (n >= 2) {
   215	          nak[p[1]] = p[2]
   216	        }
   217	      }
   218	      close(na)
   219	    }
   220	    BEGIN { load_mapped(); load_na() }
   221	    {
   222	      key=$1; name=$2
   223	      status="MISSING"; tgt=""; meth=""; reason=""
   224	      if (key in mapk) {
   225	        status="REVIEWED"
   226	        tgt=mapk[key]
   227	      } else if (key in nak) {
   228	        status="N/A"
   229	        reason=nak[key]
   230	      }
   231	      # split cs_target back into file vs method for the tsv output
   232	      if (status == "REVIEWED") {
   233	        n=split(tgt, parts, " ")
   234	        printf("%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t\n", key, name, status, parts[1], parts[2])
   235	      } else {
   236	        printf("%s\t%s\t%s\t\t\t%s\n", key, name, status, reason)
   237	      }
   238	    }
   239	  ' "$TMP_UPSTREAM"
   240	} > "$TSV_OUT"
   241	
   242	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
   243	# Step 5. Render markdown.
   244	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
   245	
   246	{
   247	  echo "# Coverage map — upstream test ↔ C# test"
   248	  echo ""
   249	  echo "Generated by [\`coverage-map.sh\`](./coverage-map.sh)."
   250	  echo ""
   251	  echo "Statuses:"
   252	  echo ""
   253	  echo "- **REVIEWED** — at least one C# \`[Fact]/[Theory]\` method has a"
   254	  echo "  matching \`/// upstream: <relpath>:<line>\` annotation."
   255	  echo "- **N/A** — the upstream test is intentionally not ported; the reason"
   256	  echo "  is recorded in [\`coverage-map.na.tsv\`](./coverage-map.na.tsv)."
   257	  echo "- **MISSING** — neither of the above. PR-3 must either add C# coverage"
   258	  echo "  or add an explicit N/A row with a non-empty reason."
   259	  echo ""
   260	  echo "## Summary"

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba codex-crypto-reviews/coverage-map.sh | sed -n '260,380p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4
 succeeded in 0ms:
   260	  echo "## Summary"
   261	  echo ""
   262	  awk -F '\t' 'NR>1 { c[$3]++ } END { for (k in c) printf("- %s: %d\n", k, c[k]) }' "$TSV_OUT" | sort
   263	  echo ""
   264	  echo "## Rows"
   265	  echo ""
   266	  echo "| Upstream key | Upstream test name | Status | C# target | C# method | N/A reason |"
   267	  echo "|---|---|---|---|---|---|"
   268	  awk -F '\t' 'NR>1 {
   269	    # escape pipes in names just in case
   270	    for (i=1; i<=NF; i++) gsub(/\|/, "\\|", $i)
   271	    printf("| `%s` | %s | %s | %s | %s | %s |\n", $1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)
   272	  }' "$TSV_OUT"
   273	} > "$MD_OUT"
   274	
   275	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
   276	# Step 6. --check mode: enforce gate.
   277	# -------------------------------------------------------------------
   278	
   279	if [ "$CHECK_MODE" = "1" ]; then
   280	  exit_code=0
   281	  # any MISSING ?
   282	  if awk -F '\t' 'NR>1 && $3=="MISSING" { print; found=1 } END { exit found?1:0 }' "$TSV_OUT" >/dev/null; then
   283	    : # awk exit 0 means no missing
   284	  else
   285	    echo "ERROR: MISSING rows detected. See $TSV_OUT or $MD_OUT."
   286	    awk -F '\t' 'NR>1 && $3=="MISSING" { print "  MISSING: " $1 "  " $2 }' "$TSV_OUT"
   287	    exit_code=1
   288	  fi
   289	  # any N/A with empty reason ?
   290	  if awk -F '\t' 'NR>1 && $3=="N/A" && ($6=="" || $6 ~ /^[[:space:]]*$/) { print; found=1 } END { exit found?1:0 }' "$TSV_OUT" >/dev/null; then
   291	    :
   292	  else
   293	    echo "ERROR: N/A rows missing reason text:"
   294	    awk -F '\t' 'NR>1 && $3=="N/A" && ($6=="" || $6 ~ /^[[:space:]]*$/) { print "  EMPTY-NA-REASON: " $1 "  " $2 }' "$TSV_OUT"
   295	    exit_code=1
   296	  fi
   297	  if [ "$exit_code" = "0" ]; then
   298	    echo "coverage-map check passed: 0 MISSING, all N/A rows have reasons."
   299	  fi
   300	  exit "$exit_code"
   301	fi
   302	
   303	# Default mode prints a short summary to stderr so CI logs are readable.
   304	{
   305	  echo "Wrote $TSV_OUT"
   306	  echo "Wrote $MD_OUT"
   307	  awk -F '\t' 'NR>1 { c[$3]++ } END { for (k in c) printf("  %s: %d\n", k, c[k]) }' "$TSV_OUT" | sort
   308	} 1>&2

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md | sed -n '540,620p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4
 succeeded in 0ms:
   540	    "npm_lockfile_sha256": "...",
   541	    "output_sha256": "...",
   542	    "note": "@turnkey/http@3.16.0 + @turnkey/api-key-stamper@0.5.0 (purejs) で生成。stamp の signature は RFC 6979 deterministic-k なため byte 一意。"
   543	  },
   544	  "stampGetWhoami": [
   545	    {
   546	      "input": { "organizationId": "<UUID>", "baseUrl": "https://api.turnkey.com", "apiPrivateKey": "<hex>", "apiPublicKey": "<hex>" },
   547	      "expectedSignedRequest": {
   548	        "url": "...",
   549	        "body": "...",         // JSON.stringify そのもの (改行・スペース無し)
   550	        "stampHeaderName": "X-Stamp",
   551	        "stampHeaderValue": "<base64url>"
   552	      }
   553	    }
   554	  ],
   555	  "stampInitImportPrivateKey": [ /* ... */ ],
   556	  "stampImportPrivateKey":     [ /* ... */ ],
   557	  "stampExportPrivateKey":     [ /* ... */ ],
   558	  "stampExportWalletAccount":  [ /* ... */ ]
   559	}
   560	```
   561	
   562	C# 側 (`HttpTests.cs`) の追加 assert:
   563	- `signedRequest.body` (UTF-8 bytes) が**完全一致**。
   564	- `signedRequest.stampHeaderValue` を base64url-decode → JSON 復元 →
   565	  キー順 `["publicKey","scheme","signature"]` 一致。
   566	- `signature` の DER 16 進文字列が**完全一致** (purejs deterministic 前提)。
   567	
   568	これにより、既存の `HttpTests.Stamp_HeaderValueDecodesAndVerifies` の
   569	「DER 形式と検証だけ」というギャップ
   570	([Http.cs-codex-findings-reconciliation.md F](../codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-codex-findings-reconciliation.md))
   571	を埋める。
   572	
   573	## 7. 実装計画 (PR 単位)
   574	
   575	| # | PR タイトル | 内容 | 依存 | サイズ |
   576	|---|---|---|---|---|
   577	| PR-1 | docs: equivalence verification design v3 | 本ファイル `plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md` を commit。Codex plan review を review-clean になるまで反復 | なし | S |
   578	| PR-1b | docs: peak monorepo usage grep evidence | `codex-crypto-reviews/peak-usage-grep-evidence.md` を作成。peak のソースコードに対し以下を grep し結果をコミット: (a) `Turnkey.Crypto.Proofs` / `verifyAppProof` / `proof.ts` の呼び出し、(b) `withAsyncPolling` の利用、(c) `hpkeAuthEncrypt` の利用、(d) `extractPrivateKeyFromPKCS8Bytes` の利用、(e) QOS `quorumKeyEncrypt` の利用。各 grep 結果 (0 件 / >0 件) を文書化 | PR-1 | S |
   579	| PR-2 | tests: upstream test-name coverage matrix (gate なし) | `coverage-map.sh` + `coverage-map.md` を追加。既存 C# テストに `/// upstream:` xml-doc を付ける。**この PR では `MISSING` 行を許容**し、`coverage-map.md` に documented リストとして残す。CI gate は **PR-3 で enable** する | PR-1, PR-1b | M |
   580	| PR-3 | tests: missing C# tests + fail-on-MISSING gate enable | PR-2 で `MISSING` だった上流 test に対応する C# テストを追加。**追加できないもの (`hpkeAuthEncrypt`、`withAsyncPolling`、proof verifier、`extractPrivateKeyFromPKCS8Bytes`、`verifyRequestStamp` 等) は `coverage-map.tsv` で N/A 理由付きで documented** とし、それ以降は `MISSING` 行ゼロ + `N/A` 理由必須を CI で fail-on にする。テストのみで `src/*.cs` は触らない。N/A 判定根拠は PR-1b の grep evidence に依拠 | PR-1b, PR-2 | M〜L |
   581	| PR-4 | tests: Node fixture generator scaffolding | `tests/Fixtures/Generators/` ディレクトリ、`package.json` (pinned)、`package-lock.json`、README、`.gitattributes` (LF)、**`tests/turnkey-sdk-csharp.Tests.csproj` の `Fixtures/**/*` ItemGroup に `Exclude="Fixtures/Generators/**"` と `<Content Remove="Fixtures/Generators/**" />` を追加して `dotnet build` から exclusion** (Section 6.2 末尾) | PR-1 | S |
   582	| PR-5 | tests: Node-generated HTTP byte vectors | `generate-http-vectors.mjs` + `turnkey-http-vectors.json` + `HttpTests.cs` 追加 assert | PR-4 | M |
   583	| PR-6 | tests: Node-generated stamper byte vectors | `generate-stamper-vectors.mjs` + `turnkey-stamper-node-vectors.json` + `ApiKeyStamperTests.cs` 追加 assert | PR-4 | M |
   584	| PR-7 | tests: Node-generated HPKE round-trip + structural fixtures | encrypt → decrypt round-trip で plaintext 等価、encrypt 出力構造 (encapsulated key 長等) を assert。**encrypt の byte snapshot は取らない** (Section 2.1 の非決定論経路) | PR-4 | M |
   585	| PR-7a | research: SDK HPKE ↔ RFC 9180 byte-equality 評価 | `tests/Fixtures/Generators/hpke-rfc9180-eval.mjs` で固定 ephemeral key 注入経路を探索し、`codex-crypto-reviews/sdk-hpke-vs-rfc9180.md` に結論。byte-equal なら PR-9b に進む、equal でないなら見送る | PR-4 | M |
   586	| PR-8 | tests: Primitives standard vectors (HKDF/ECDSA/HMAC/Ed25519/secp256k1/Base58Check) | `tests/Fixtures/rfc/{hkdf,ecdsa-p256-rfc6979,hmac-sha256-rfc4231,ed25519-rfc8032}/`、`tests/Fixtures/sec/secp256k1-sec2/`、`tests/Fixtures/bitcoin/base58check-bip39/` + 対応 test 追加。**同 PR で `tests/Fixtures/README.md` の "Provenance levels" 章に `sec` (= "sec2-published") と `bitcoin` (= "bip-test-vectors") を新規 level として追加 + Layout 例を更新** | なし (PR-1 後すぐ可) | M |
   587	| PR-9 | tests: Primitives NIST vectors (AES-GCM-256/SHA-256/ECDSA SigVer) | `tests/Fixtures/nist/` 整備 (AES-GCM **256 のみ**、SDK 経路で使われない 128 は取り込まない) | PR-8 | M |
   588	| PR-9b | tests: HPKE RFC 9180 vectors (条件付き) | **PR-7a が byte-equal を確認した場合のみ実施**。RFC 9180 Appendix A.4 vectors を取り込む | PR-7a, PR-8 | M |
   589	| PR-10 | tests: 他言語 SDK 共有ハードコード値の cross-language 借用 | `mockSenderPrivateKey`, `mockCredentialBundle`, ニーモニック等を C# で同名定数化、対応テスト追加 | PR-3 | S |
   590	| PR-11 | tests: proof fixtures + provenance assertion (verify 本体は scope 外) | `tests/Fixtures/proofs/` に `proof-tests.ts` から AppProof / BootProof JSON を借用しコミット。**`ProofFixtureProvenanceTests` を一緒に追加**: JSON parse、`_provenance` schema validation、上流 sha256 一致を assert。verify ロジックの C# 実装は別計画 | PR-1 | S |
   591	| PR-12 | ci: upstream-drift detection workflow (idempotent) | 月次 cron、上流テスト sha256 比較、差分時に **同 label + パッケージ名で 1 issue を update or create** (Section 6.4 yaml 例) | PR-2 | S |
   592	| PR-13 | ci: fixture regeneration workflow (opt-in) | `workflow_dispatch` + 月次 cron で Generators を回し、diff を PR | PR-4〜7 | S |
   593	| PR-13b | ci: setup-node 追加 (PR-13 内) | `.github/workflows/fixture-regen.yml` に `actions/setup-node@v4` を追加。**main CI (`ci.yml` / `release.yml`) は触らない** (Codex r2 で確認: 既存 CI は .NET のみで Node に触らない) | PR-13 と同 PR でも可 | S |
   594	| PR-14 | docs: README "Verification posture" + NOTICE 更新 | 同等性検証の到達点と前提を README に明記、他言語 SDK 借用箇所の attribution、proof scope 外を明記 | PR-9, PR-10, PR-11, PR-12, PR-13 全部 + (PR-7a が byte-equal を確認した場合は) **PR-9b 完了後** | S |
   595	
   596	PR-1 (本プラン commit) を最初に Codex review-clean まで反復してから、
   597	PR-2 以降は依存順に進める。**全 PR が独立にコミット可能** で、
   598	途中で打ち切ってもリポジトリは壊れない。
   599	
   600	## 8. CI / 完了基準
   601	
   602	ローカルおよび GitHub Actions で:
   603	
   604	1. `dotnet test -c Release` がすべてグリーン。
   605	2. `codex-crypto-reviews/coverage-map.sh --check` が exit 0
   606	   (`MISSING` 行ゼロ、空 N/A もブロック)。
   607	3. `upstream-drift.yml` の最新月次実行が成功 (= 差分なし、
   608	   または **同じ open issue で update され続けている** 状態)。
   609	4. `FINAL-INTEGRATED-REVIEW-*.md` の更新版が同条件で再 GO。
   610	5. README "Verification posture" 章が **PR-14 で更新され main にマージ済み**。
   611	6. Tier-2 fixtures の決定論経路は **byte-equal**、非決定論経路は
   612	   **decrypt round-trip 等価 + 構造的 assertion パス**。
   613	7. (PR-7a が byte-equal を確認した場合のみ) HPKE RFC 9180 vectors を C# 実装が **byte-equal** で通る。
   614	
   615	## 9. リスクと対策
   616	
   617	| リスク | 対策 |
   618	|---|---|
   619	| `purejs` runtime と `nodecrypto.ts` runtime の signature byte 差 | PR-5/6 では `runtimeOverride: "purejs"` を必ず指定。doc にも明記。さらに generator pre-flight で既知 vector check |
   620	| Node version drift で fixture が再生成毎に変わる | `package-lock.json` を commit、`node_version` を fixture に記録、generator が `process.version` を起動時 assert |

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .github/workflows/ci.yml | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	name: CI
     2	
     3	on:
     4	  push:
     5	    branches: [main]
     6	  pull_request:
     7	    branches: [main]
     8	
     9	permissions:
    10	  contents: read
    11	
    12	jobs:
    13	  build-test:
    14	    name: Build + test (ubuntu-latest, net8.0 runner)
    15	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    16	    steps:
    17	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
    18	
    19	      - name: Setup .NET 8
    20	        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v5
    21	        with:
    22	          dotnet-version: 8.0.x
    23	
    24	      - name: Verify packages.lock.json is present
    25	        run: |
    26	          test -f src/packages.lock.json   || (echo "src/packages.lock.json missing"   && exit 1)
    27	          test -f tests/packages.lock.json || (echo "tests/packages.lock.json missing" && exit 1)
    28	
    29	      - name: Restore (locked-mode)
    30	        run: dotnet restore turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln --locked-mode
    31	
    32	      - name: Build (Release, both TFMs)
    33	        run: dotnet build turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln -c Release --no-restore
    34	
    35	      - name: Test (Release, with coverage)
    36	        env:
    37	          # E2E tests skip themselves if these are unset
    38	          TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_API_KEY }}
    39	          TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_ID: ${{ secrets.TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_ID }}
    40	        run: |
    41	          dotnet test turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln -c Release --no-build \
    42	            --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" \
    43	            --results-directory ./TestResults \
    44	            --logger "trx;LogFileName=test-results.trx"
    45	
    46	      # Equivalence-verification gate: every upstream __tests__/*.ts test()
    47	      # block must either map to a C# [Fact]/[Theory] via a /// upstream:
    48	      # XML doc annotation, or be listed in coverage-map.na.tsv with a
    49	      # non-empty reason. Enabled in PR-3 per plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md.
    50	      - name: Coverage map gate (upstream test ↔ C# test)
    51	        run: ./codex-crypto-reviews/coverage-map.sh --check
    52	
    53	      # Coverage gate. Threshold is set to 30% combined-TFM for
    54	      # v0.1.0-alpha because the report counts both the netstandard2.1
    55	      # and net8.0 production assemblies (tests only target net8.0) AND
    56	      # the System.Text.Json source-generated TypeInfo classes (most of
    57	      # which are never executed by tests). The local single-TFM
    58	      # net8.0-only figure is ~78% line.
    59	      # TODOs to raise back:
    60	      #   1. Filter the cobertura output to net8.0 only (or unify reports).
    61	      #   2. Add targeted negative tests for
    62	      #      Crypto.DecryptCredentialBundle / EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle /
    63	      #      DecryptExportBundle error paths once Turnkey sample bundles
    64	      #      are committable.
    65	      - name: Coverage gate (>=30% combined-TFM line for v0.1.0-alpha)
    66	        uses: irongut/CodeCoverageSummary@v1.3.0
    67	        with:
    68	          filename: TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml
    69	          badge: false
    70	          fail_below_min: true
    71	          thresholds: '30 50'
    72	          format: markdown
    73	          output: both
    74	
    75	      - name: Upload coverage artifact
    76	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
    77	        if: always()
    78	        with:
    79	          name: coverage-report
    80	          path: TestResults/**
    81	
    82	  pack:
    83	    name: dotnet pack (validation)
    84	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    85	    needs: build-test
    86	    steps:
    87	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
    88	      - uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v5
    89	        with:
    90	          dotnet-version: 8.0.x
    91	      - name: Restore (locked-mode)
    92	        run: dotnet restore turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln --locked-mode
    93	      - name: Pack (Release)
    94	        run: |
    95	          dotnet pack src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj -c Release \
    96	            --no-restore --output ./artifacts
    97	      - name: Validate NuGet contents (README, LICENSE, NOTICE)
    98	        run: |
    99	          set -euo pipefail
   100	          NUPKG=$(ls ./artifacts/KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk.*.nupkg | head -1)
   101	          echo "Validating $NUPKG"
   102	          unzip -l "$NUPKG" | tee /tmp/contents.txt
   103	          grep -q 'README.md' /tmp/contents.txt
   104	          grep -q 'LICENSE'   /tmp/contents.txt
   105	          grep -q 'NOTICE'    /tmp/contents.txt
   106	      - name: Upload .nupkg + .snupkg artifacts
   107	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
   108	        with:
   109	          name: nupkgs
   110	          path: ./artifacts/**

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .github/workflows/upstream-drift.yml | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Upstream-drift detection (idempotent).
     2	#
     3	# Once a month, hash each pinned upstream __tests__/*.ts and
     4	# __fixtures__/* file at the pinned git tag SHA via the GitHub
     5	# Contents API, then compare to the local pin in
     6	# codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/test-file-checksums.txt.
     7	#
     8	# Per plan Section 6.4 and Section 9 risk row "公式テストファイル
     9	# sha256 を pin したのに毎月差分が出て CI が騒がしい":
    10	#
    11	#   - This workflow is NOT a main-branch CI gate. It can never block
    12	#     a PR. Drift surfaces only as a GitHub issue.
    13	#   - Issue creation is idempotent: at most ONE open issue with the
    14	#     `upstream-drift` label and the package name in the title. A
    15	#     re-detected drift updates the body and posts a re-detection
    16	#     comment instead of opening a duplicate.
    17	#
    18	# Manual trigger via workflow_dispatch is supported so reviewers can
    19	# re-run after re-pinning without waiting for the next cron tick.
    20	
    21	name: Upstream drift detection
    22	
    23	on:
    24	  workflow_dispatch:
    25	  schedule:
    26	    - cron: "0 8 1 * *"  # 1st of each month, 08:00 UTC
    27	
    28	permissions:
    29	  contents: read
    30	  issues: write
    31	
    32	jobs:
    33	  drift:
    34	    name: Drift detection (${{ matrix.package }})
    35	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    36	    strategy:
    37	      fail-fast: false
    38	      matrix:
    39	        include:
    40	          - package: turnkey-crypto-2.8.8
    41	            owner: tkhq
    42	            repo: sdk
    43	            git_tag_sha: b35dc642bd7c1728f97acd43d4cba66976b65084
    44	            tarball_dir: packages/crypto
    45	          - package: turnkey-http-3.16.0
    46	            owner: tkhq
    47	            repo: sdk
    48	            git_tag_sha: 8def9ba521233137437ac7294693a4ae0a0d14da
    49	            tarball_dir: packages/http
    50	          - package: turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0
    51	            owner: tkhq
    52	            repo: sdk
    53	            git_tag_sha: b711befbb88ec522452dbdac68f0e98762be10dd
    54	            tarball_dir: packages/api-key-stamper
    55	          - package: turnkey-encoding-0.6.0
    56	            owner: tkhq
    57	            repo: sdk
    58	            git_tag_sha: 60a997f4c52ac5f98bdd285af934f02699b88bff
    59	            tarball_dir: packages/encoding
    60	    steps:
    61	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
    62	
    63	      # The pin file format is "<sha256>  <relpath>" where <relpath>
    64	      # always starts with "codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/".
    65	      # We need to convert that to the GitHub Contents API path which
    66	      # is "<tarball_dir>/src/__tests__/<file>" — the live upstream
    67	      # GitHub tree does not have a "ts-source" prefix.
    68	      #
    69	      # Concretely the local path looks like:
    70	      #   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/ts-source/__tests__/crypto-test.ts
    71	      # and the upstream path is:
    72	      #   packages/crypto/src/__tests__/crypto-test.ts
    73	      #
    74	      # The mapping is per-package because monorepo layouts vary.
    75	      - name: Compute drift report
    76	        id: drift
    77	        env:
    78	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    79	          PACKAGE: ${{ matrix.package }}
    80	          OWNER: ${{ matrix.owner }}
    81	          REPO: ${{ matrix.repo }}
    82	          GIT_SHA: ${{ matrix.git_tag_sha }}
    83	          TARBALL_DIR: ${{ matrix.tarball_dir }}
    84	        run: |
    85	          set -euo pipefail
    86	
    87	          REPORT=$(mktemp)
    88	          : > "$REPORT"
    89	
    90	          drift_count=0
    91	
    92	          while IFS= read -r line; do
    93	            # Skip comments and blank lines.
    94	            [[ -z "${line// }" ]] && continue
    95	            [[ "$line" =~ ^# ]] && continue
    96	
    97	            # Format: "<sha256>  <relpath>"
    98	            expected_sha=$(awk '{print $1}' <<<"$line")
    99	            relpath=$(awk '{print $2}' <<<"$line")
   100	
   101	            # Filter to this matrix package only.
   102	            case "$relpath" in
   103	              "codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/${PACKAGE}/"*) ;;
   104	              *) continue ;;
   105	            esac
   106	
   107	            # Strip the local prefix to derive the in-tarball relative path,
   108	            # then rewrite "ts-source" -> "src" for the live upstream layout.
   109	            local_subpath="${relpath#codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/${PACKAGE}/}"
   110	            upstream_subpath=${local_subpath/ts-source\//src/}
   111	
   112	            # Read the live file via the Contents API at the pinned SHA.
   113	            api_path="repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/contents/${TARBALL_DIR}/${upstream_subpath}?ref=${GIT_SHA}"
   114	            live_b64=$(gh api -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" "$api_path" --jq '.content // ""')
   115	            if [[ -z "$live_b64" ]]; then
   116	              {
   117	                echo "- **MISSING_UPSTREAM**: \`${upstream_subpath}\` (not found at ${OWNER}/${REPO}@${GIT_SHA})";
   118	              } >> "$REPORT"
   119	              drift_count=$((drift_count + 1))
   120	              continue
   121	            fi
   122	
   123	            # Contents API base64-decodes with embedded newlines.
   124	            live_sha=$(printf '%s' "$live_b64" | tr -d '\n' | base64 -d | shasum -a 256 | awk '{print $1}')
   125	
   126	            if [[ "$live_sha" != "$expected_sha" ]]; then
   127	              {
   128	                echo "- **DRIFT**: \`${upstream_subpath}\``"
   129	                echo "  - expected sha256 \`${expected_sha}\``"
   130	                echo "  - live sha256     \`${live_sha}\``"
   131	                echo "  - live ref        \`${OWNER}/${REPO}@${GIT_SHA}\``"
   132	              } >> "$REPORT"
   133	              drift_count=$((drift_count + 1))
   134	            fi
   135	          done < codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/test-file-checksums.txt
   136	
   137	          {
   138	            echo "DRIFT_COUNT=$drift_count"
   139	            echo "REPORT_PATH=$REPORT"
   140	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   141	
   142	      # Idempotent issue handling (plan Section 6.4):
   143	      #   - Search for an open issue labelled `upstream-drift` whose
   144	      #     title contains the package name.
   145	      #   - If found: edit the body to the latest report and add a
   146	      #     re-detection comment with the timestamp.
   147	      #   - If not found: open a new issue.
   148	      #   - If drift_count is 0 and an open issue exists: close it
   149	      #     with a "resolved" comment.
   150	      - name: Upsert drift issue
   151	        if: always()
   152	        env:
   153	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
   154	          DRIFT_LABEL: upstream-drift
   155	          PACKAGE: ${{ matrix.package }}
   156	          DRIFT_COUNT: ${{ steps.drift.outputs.DRIFT_COUNT }}
   157	          REPORT_PATH: ${{ steps.drift.outputs.REPORT_PATH }}
   158	        run: |
   159	          set -euo pipefail
   160	
   161	          gh label create "$DRIFT_LABEL" --color "d73a4a" \
   162	            --description "Upstream test-file sha256 drift detected" 2>/dev/null || true
   163	
   164	          # Build the body up-front so the rest of the logic is single-shot.
   165	          if [[ "$DRIFT_COUNT" -gt 0 ]]; then
   166	            BODY_FILE=$(mktemp)
   167	            {
   168	              echo "Detected $DRIFT_COUNT drift row(s) for package \`$PACKAGE\` at $(date -u +%FT%TZ)."
   169	              echo ""
   170	              echo "## Drift"
   171	              echo ""
   172	              cat "$REPORT_PATH"
   173	              echo ""
   174	              echo "## Re-pin procedure"
   175	              echo ""
   176	              echo "See \`codex-crypto-reviews/turnkey-source-pins.md\` (\"Re-pinning procedure\")."
   177	            } > "$BODY_FILE"
   178	          fi
   179	
   180	          # Locate the single existing open issue (idempotent).
   181	          EXISTING=$(gh issue list \
   182	            --state open \
   183	            --label "$DRIFT_LABEL" \
   184	            --search "in:title $PACKAGE" \
   185	            --json number \
   186	            --jq '.[0].number // empty')
   187	
   188	          if [[ "$DRIFT_COUNT" -gt 0 ]]; then
   189	            if [[ -n "$EXISTING" ]]; then
   190	              gh issue edit "$EXISTING" --body-file "$BODY_FILE"
   191	              gh issue comment "$EXISTING" --body \
   192	                "Re-detected upstream drift on $(date -u +%FT%TZ)."
   193	            else
   194	              gh issue create \
   195	                --label "$DRIFT_LABEL" \
   196	                --title "Upstream drift: $PACKAGE" \
   197	                --body-file "$BODY_FILE"
   198	            fi
   199	          else
   200	            if [[ -n "$EXISTING" ]]; then
   201	              gh issue comment "$EXISTING" --body \
   202	                "Drift resolved (re-checked $(date -u +%FT%TZ))."
   203	              gh issue close "$EXISTING"
   204	            else
   205	              echo "No drift detected and no open issue. Nothing to do."
   206	            fi
   207	          fi

 succeeded in 489ms:

     7	in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights

--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/bs58check/README.md
     1	# bs58check
     2	
     3	[![NPM Package](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/bs58check.svg?style=flat-square)](https://www.npmjs.org/package/bs58check)
     4	[![Build Status](https://img.shields.io/travis/bitcoinjs/bs58check.svg?branch=master&style=flat-square)](https://travis-ci.org/bitcoinjs/bs58check)
     5	[![Dependency status](https://img.shields.io/david/bitcoinjs/bs58check.svg?style=flat-square)](https://david-dm.org/bitcoinjs/bs58check#info=dependencies)
     6	
     7	[![js-standard-style](https://cdn.rawgit.com/feross/standard/master/badge.svg)](https://github.com/feross/standard)
     8	
     9	A straight forward implementation of base58check extending upon bs58.
    10	
    11	
    12	## Example
    13	
    14	```javascript
    15	import bs58check from 'bs58check'
    16	
    17	var decoded = bs58check.decode('5Kd3NBUAdUnhyzenEwVLy9pBKxSwXvE9FMPyR4UKZvpe6E3AgLr')
    18	
    19	console.log(decoded)
    20	// => <Buffer 80 ed db dc 11 68 f1 da ea db d3 e4 4c 1e 3f 8f 5a 28 4c 20 29 f7 8a d2 6a f9 85 83 a4 99 de 5b 19>
    21	
    22	console.log(bs58check.encode(decoded))
    23	// => 5Kd3NBUAdUnhyzenEwVLy9pBKxSwXvE9FMPyR4UKZvpe6E3AgLr
    24	```
    25	
    26	
    27	## LICENSE [MIT](LICENSE)
--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/bs58check/package.json
     1	{
     2	  "name": "bs58check",
     3	  "version": "4.0.0",
     4	  "description": "A straightforward implementation of base58-check encoding",
     5	  "type": "module",
     6	  "keywords": [
     7	    "base",
     8	    "base58",
     9	    "base58check",
    10	    "bitcoin",
    11	    "bs58",
    12	    "check",
    13	    "checksum",
    14	    "decode",
    15	    "decoding",
    16	    "encode",
    17	    "encoding",
    18	    "litecoin"
    19	  ],
    20	  "homepage": "https://github.com/bitcoinjs/bs58check",
    21	  "bugs": {
    22	    "url": "https://github.com/bitcoinjs/bs58check/issues"
    23	  },
    24	  "license": "MIT",
    25	  "author": "Daniel Cousens",
    26	  "files": [
    27	    "src"
    28	  ],
    29	  "main": "src/cjs/index.cjs",
    30	  "module": "src/esm/index.js",
    31	  "types": "src/cjs/index.d.ts",
    32	  "exports": {
    33	    ".": {
    34	      "require": "./src/cjs/index.cjs",
    35	      "import": "./src/esm/index.js",
    36	      "types": "./src/cjs/index.d.ts"
    37	    }
    38	  },
    39	  "repository": {
    40	    "type": "git",
    41	    "url": "https://github.com/bitcoinjs/bs58check.git"
    42	  },
    43	  "scripts": {
    44	    "build": "npm run clean && tsc -p ./tsconfig.json && tsc -p ./tsconfig.cjs.json",
    45	    "clean": "rimraf src",
    46	    "gitdiff": "npm run build && git diff --exit-code",
    47	    "postbuild": "chmod +x ./fixup.sh && ./fixup.sh",
    48	    "coverage-report": "c8 report --reporter=lcov",
    49	    "coverage": "c8 --check-coverage --branches 90 --functions 90 npm run unit",
    50	    "standard": "ts-standard --ignore test --ignore src",
    51	    "test": "npm run standard -- --fix && npm run coverage",
    52	    "unit": "tape test/*.js"
    53	  },
    54	  "dependencies": {
    55	    "@noble/hashes": "^1.2.0",
    56	    "bs58": "^6.0.0"
    57	  },
    58	  "devDependencies": {
    59	    "@types/node": "^20.14.8",
    60	    "blake-hash": "^1.0.0",
    61	    "c8": "^10.1.2",
    62	    "rimraf": "^5.0.7",
    63	    "safe-buffer": "^5.1.2",
    64	    "standard": "^17.1.0",
    65	    "tape": "^5.3.0",
    66	    "ts-standard": "^12.0.2",
    67	    "typescript": "^5.5.2"
    68	  }
    69	}

--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/cbor-js/README.md
     1	cbor-js
     2	=======
     3	
     4	The Concise Binary Object Representation (CBOR) data format ([RFC 7049](http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7049)) implemented in pure JavaScript.
     5	
     6	[![Build Status](https://api.travis-ci.org/paroga/cbor-js.png)](https://travis-ci.org/paroga/cbor-js)
     7	[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/paroga/cbor-js/badge.png?branch=master)](https://coveralls.io/r/paroga/cbor-js?branch=master)
     8	[![Dependency status](https://david-dm.org/paroga/cbor-js/status.png)](https://david-dm.org/paroga/cbor-js#info=dependencies&view=table)
     9	[![Dev Dependency Status](https://david-dm.org/paroga/cbor-js/dev-status.png)](https://david-dm.org/paroga/cbor-js#info=devDependencies&view=table)
    10	[![Selenium Test Status](https://saucelabs.com/buildstatus/paroga-cbor-js)](https://saucelabs.com/u/paroga-cbor-js)
    11	
    12	[![Selenium Test Status](https://saucelabs.com/browser-matrix/paroga-cbor-js.svg)](https://saucelabs.com/u/paroga-cbor-js)
    13	
    14	API
    15	---
    16	
    17	The `CBOR`-object provides the following two functions:
    18	
    19	CBOR.**decode**(*data*)
    20	> Take the ArrayBuffer object *data* and return it decoded as a JavaScript object.
    21	
    22	CBOR.**encode**(*data*)
    23	> Take the JavaScript object *data* and return it encoded as a ArrayBuffer object.
    24	
    25	Usage
    26	-----
    27	
    28	Include `cbor.js` in your or HTML page:
    29	```html
    30	<script src="path/to/cbor.js" type="text/javascript"></script>
    31	```
    32	
    33	Then you can use it via the `CBOR`-object in your code:
    34	```javascript
    35	var initial = { Hello: "World" };
    36	var encoded = CBOR.encode(initial);
    37	var decoded = CBOR.decode(encoded);
    38	```
    39	After running this example `initial` and `decoded` represent the same value.
    40	
    41	### Combination with WebSocket
    42	
    43	The API was designed to play well with the `WebSocket` object in the browser:
    44	```javascript
    45	var websocket = new WebSocket(url);
    46	websocket.binaryType = "arraybuffer";
    47	...
    48	websocket.onmessage = function(event) {
    49	  var message = CBOR.decode(event.data);
    50	};
    51	...
    52	websocket.send(CBOR.encode(message));
    53	```
--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/cbor-js/cbor.js
     1	/*
     2	 * The MIT License (MIT)
     3	 *
     4	 * Copyright (c) 2014 Patrick Gansterer <paroga@paroga.com>
     5	 *
     6	 * Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy
     7	 * of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal
     8	 * in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights
     9	 * to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell
    10	 * copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is
    11	 * furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions:
    12	 *
    13	 * The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all
    14	 * copies or substantial portions of the Software.
    15	 *
    16	 * THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR
    17	 * IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY,
    18	 * FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE
    19	 * AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER
    20	 * LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM,
    21	 * OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE
    22	 * SOFTWARE.
    23	 */
    24	
    25	(function(global, undefined) { "use strict";
    26	var POW_2_24 = Math.pow(2, -24),
    27	    POW_2_32 = Math.pow(2, 32),
    28	    POW_2_53 = Math.pow(2, 53);
    29	
    30	function encode(value) {
    31	  var data = new ArrayBuffer(256);
    32	  var dataView = new DataView(data);
    33	  var lastLength;
    34	  var offset = 0;
    35	
    36	  function ensureSpace(length) {
    37	    var newByteLength = data.byteLength;
    38	    var requiredLength = offset + length;
    39	    while (newByteLength < requiredLength)
    40	      newByteLength *= 2;
    41	    if (newByteLength !== data.byteLength) {
    42	      var oldDataView = dataView;
    43	      data = new ArrayBuffer(newByteLength);
    44	      dataView = new DataView(data);
    45	      var uint32count = (offset + 3) >> 2;
    46	      for (var i = 0; i < uint32count; ++i)
    47	        dataView.setUint32(i * 4, oldDataView.getUint32(i * 4));
    48	    }
    49	
    50	    lastLength = length;
    51	    return dataView;
    52	  }
    53	  function write() {
    54	    offset += lastLength;
    55	  }
    56	  function writeFloat64(value) {
    57	    write(ensureSpace(8).setFloat64(offset, value));
    58	  }
    59	  function writeUint8(value) {
    60	    write(ensureSpace(1).setUint8(offset, value));
    61	  }
    62	  function writeUint8Array(value) {
    63	    var dataView = ensureSpace(value.length);
    64	    for (var i = 0; i < value.length; ++i)
    65	      dataView.setUint8(offset + i, value[i]);
    66	    write();
    67	  }
    68	  function writeUint16(value) {
    69	    write(ensureSpace(2).setUint16(offset, value));
    70	  }
    71	  function writeUint32(value) {
    72	    write(ensureSpace(4).setUint32(offset, value));
    73	  }
    74	  function writeUint64(value) {
    75	    var low = value % POW_2_32;
    76	    var high = (value - low) / POW_2_32;
    77	    var dataView = ensureSpace(8);
    78	    dataView.setUint32(offset, high);
    79	    dataView.setUint32(offset + 4, low);
    80	    write();
    81	  }
    82	  function writeTypeAndLength(type, length) {
    83	    if (length < 24) {
    84	      writeUint8(type << 5 | length);
    85	    } else if (length < 0x100) {
    86	      writeUint8(type << 5 | 24);
    87	      writeUint8(length);
    88	    } else if (length < 0x10000) {
    89	      writeUint8(type << 5 | 25);
    90	      writeUint16(length);
    91	    } else if (length < 0x100000000) {
    92	      writeUint8(type << 5 | 26);
    93	      writeUint32(length);
    94	    } else {
    95	      writeUint8(type << 5 | 27);
    96	      writeUint64(length);
    97	    }
    98	  }
    99	  
   100	  function encodeItem(value) {
   101	    var i;
   102	
   103	    if (value === false)
   104	      return writeUint8(0xf4);
   105	    if (value === true)
   106	      return writeUint8(0xf5);
   107	    if (value === null)
   108	      return writeUint8(0xf6);
   109	    if (value === undefined)
   110	      return writeUint8(0xf7);
   111	  
   112	    switch (typeof value) {
   113	      case "number":
   114	        if (Math.floor(value) === value) {
   115	          if (0 <= value && value <= POW_2_53)
   116	            return writeTypeAndLength(0, value);
   117	          if (-POW_2_53 <= value && value < 0)
   118	            return writeTypeAndLength(1, -(value + 1));
   119	        }
   120	        writeUint8(0xfb);
   121	        return writeFloat64(value);
   122	
   123	      case "string":
   124	        var utf8data = [];
   125	        for (i = 0; i < value.length; ++i) {
   126	          var charCode = value.charCodeAt(i);
   127	          if (charCode < 0x80) {
   128	            utf8data.push(charCode);
   129	          } else if (charCode < 0x800) {
   130	            utf8data.push(0xc0 | charCode >> 6);
   131	            utf8data.push(0x80 | charCode & 0x3f);
   132	          } else if (charCode < 0xd800) {
   133	            utf8data.push(0xe0 | charCode >> 12);
   134	            utf8data.push(0x80 | (charCode >> 6)  & 0x3f);
   135	            utf8data.push(0x80 | charCode & 0x3f);
   136	          } else {
   137	            charCode = (charCode & 0x3ff) << 10;
   138	            charCode |= value.charCodeAt(++i) & 0x3ff;
   139	            charCode += 0x10000;
   140	
   141	            utf8data.push(0xf0 | charCode >> 18);
   142	            utf8data.push(0x80 | (charCode >> 12)  & 0x3f);
   143	            utf8data.push(0x80 | (charCode >> 6)  & 0x3f);
   144	            utf8data.push(0x80 | charCode & 0x3f);
   145	          }
   146	        }
   147	
   148	        writeTypeAndLength(3, utf8data.length);
   149	        return writeUint8Array(utf8data);
   150	
   151	      default:
   152	        var length;
   153	        if (Array.isArray(value)) {
   154	          length = value.length;
   155	          writeTypeAndLength(4, length);
   156	          for (i = 0; i < length; ++i)
   157	            encodeItem(value[i]);
   158	        } else if (value instanceof Uint8Array) {
   159	          writeTypeAndLength(2, value.length);
   160	          writeUint8Array(value);
   161	        } else {
   162	          var keys = Object.keys(value);
   163	          length = keys.length;
   164	          writeTypeAndLength(5, length);
   165	          for (i = 0; i < length; ++i) {
   166	            var key = keys[i];
   167	            encodeItem(key);
   168	            encodeItem(value[key]);
   169	          }
   170	        }
   171	    }
   172	  }
   173	  
   174	  encodeItem(value);
   175	
   176	  if ("slice" in data)
   177	    return data.slice(0, offset);
   178	  
   179	  var ret = new ArrayBuffer(offset);
   180	  var retView = new DataView(ret);
   181	  for (var i = 0; i < offset; ++i)
   182	    retView.setUint8(i, dataView.getUint8(i));
   183	  return ret;
   184	}
   185	
   186	function decode(data, tagger, simpleValue) {
   187	  var dataView = new DataView(data);
   188	  var offset = 0;
   189	  
   190	  if (typeof tagger !== "function")
   191	    tagger = function(value) { return value; };
   192	  if (typeof simpleValue !== "function")
   193	    simpleValue = function() { return undefined; };
   194	
   195	  function read(value, length) {
   196	    offset += length;
   197	    return value;
   198	  }
   199	  function readArrayBuffer(length) {
   200	    return read(new Uint8Array(data, offset, length), length);
   201	  }
   202	  function readFloat16() {
   203	    var tempArrayBuffer = new ArrayBuffer(4);
   204	    var tempDataView = new DataView(tempArrayBuffer);
   205	    var value = readUint16();
   206	
   207	    var sign = value & 0x8000;
   208	    var exponent = value & 0x7c00;
   209	    var fraction = value & 0x03ff;
   210	    
   211	    if (exponent === 0x7c00)
   212	      exponent = 0xff << 10;
   213	    else if (exponent !== 0)
   214	      exponent += (127 - 15) << 10;
   215	    else if (fraction !== 0)
   216	      return fraction * POW_2_24;
   217	    
   218	    tempDataView.setUint32(0, sign << 16 | exponent << 13 | fraction << 13);
   219	    return tempDataView.getFloat32(0);
   220	  }

--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/cross-fetch/README.md
     1	cross-fetch<br>
     2	[![NPM Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/cross-fetch.svg?branch=main)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/cross-fetch)
     3	[![Downloads Per Week](https://img.shields.io/npm/dw/cross-fetch.svg?color=blue)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/cross-fetch)
     4	[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
     5	[![CI](https://github.com/lquixada/cross-fetch/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/lquixada/cross-fetch/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
     6	[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/lquixada/cross-fetch/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/lquixada/cross-fetch)
     7	================
     8	
     9	Universal WHATWG Fetch API for Node, Browsers and React Native. The scenario that cross-fetch really shines is when the same JavaScript codebase needs to run on different platforms.
    10	
    11	- **Platform agnostic**: browsers, Node or React Native
    12	- **Optional polyfill**: it's up to you if something is going to be added to the global object or not
    13	- **Simple interface**: no instantiation, no configuration and no extra dependency
    14	- **WHATWG compliant**: it works the same way wherever your code runs
    15	- **TypeScript support**: better development experience with types.
    16	
    17	
    18	* * *
    19	
    20	## Table of Contents
    21	
    22	-   [Install](#install)
    23	-   [Usage](#usage)
    24	-   [Demo & API](#demo--api)
    25	-   [FAQ](#faq)
    26	-   [Thanks](#thanks)
    27	-   [License](#license)
    28	-   [Author](#author)
    29	
    30	* * *
    31	
    32	## Install
    33	
    34	```sh
    35	npm install --save cross-fetch
    36	```
    37	
    38	As a [ponyfill](https://github.com/sindresorhus/ponyfill):
    39	
    40	```javascript
    41	// Using ES6 modules with Babel or TypeScript
    42	import fetch from 'cross-fetch';
    43	
    44	// Using CommonJS modules
    45	const fetch = require('cross-fetch');
    46	```
    47	
    48	As a polyfill:
    49	
    50	```javascript
    51	// Using ES6 modules
    52	import 'cross-fetch/polyfill';
    53	
    54	// Using CommonJS modules
    55	require('cross-fetch/polyfill');
    56	```
    57	
    58	
    59	The CDN build is also available on unpkg:
    60	
    61	```html
    62	<script src="//unpkg.com/cross-fetch/dist/cross-fetch.js"></script>
    63	```
    64	
    65	This adds the fetch function to the window object. Note that this is not UMD compatible.
    66	
    67	
    68	* * *
    69	
    70	## Usage
    71	
    72	With [promises](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise):
    73	
    74	```javascript
    75	import fetch from 'cross-fetch';
    76	// Or just: import 'cross-fetch/polyfill';
    77	
    78	fetch('//api.github.com/users/lquixada')
    79	  .then(res => {
    80	    if (res.status >= 400) {
    81	      throw new Error("Bad response from server");
    82	    }
    83	    return res.json();
    84	  })
    85	  .then(user => {
    86	    console.log(user);
    87	  })
    88	  .catch(err => {
    89	    console.error(err);
    90	  });
    91	```
    92	
    93	With [async/await](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Statements/async_function):
    94	
    95	```javascript
    96	import fetch from 'cross-fetch';
    97	// Or just: import 'cross-fetch/polyfill';
    98	
    99	(async () => {
   100	  try {
   101	    const res = await fetch('//api.github.com/users/lquixada');
   102	    
   103	    if (res.status >= 400) {
   104	      throw new Error("Bad response from server");
   105	    }
   106	    
   107	    const user = await res.json();
   108	  
   109	    console.log(user);
   110	  } catch (err) {
   111	    console.error(err);
   112	  }
   113	})();
   114	```
   115	
   116	> ⚠️ **Warning**: If you're in an environment that doesn't support Promises such as Internet Explorer, you must install an ES6 Promise compatible polyfill. [es6-promise](https://github.com/jakearchibald/es6-promise) is suggested.
   117	
   118	
   119	## Demo & API
   120	
   121	You can find a comprehensive doc at [Github's fetch](https://github.github.io/fetch/) page. If you want to play with cross-fetch, check our [**JSFiddle playground**](https://jsfiddle.net/lquixada/3ypqgacp/).
   122	
   123	> **Tip**: Run the fiddle on various browsers and with different settings (for instance: cross-domain requests, wrong urls or text requests). Don't forget to open the console in the test suite page and play around.
   124	
   125	
   126	## FAQ
   127	
   128	#### Yet another fetch library?
   129	
   130	I did a lot of research in order to find a fetch library that could be simple, cross-platform and provide polyfill as an option. There's a plethora of libs out there but none could match those requirements.
   131	
   132	
   133	#### Why not isomorphic-fetch?
   134	
   135	My preferred library used to be [isomorphic-fetch](https://github.com/matthew-andrews/isomorphic-fetch) but it has this [bug](https://github.com/matthew-andrews/isomorphic-fetch/issues/125) that prevents it from running in a react native environment. It seems unlikely to be fixed since there haven't been any new commits to it since 2016. That means dependencies are outdated as well. 
   136	
   137	
   138	#### Why polyfill might not be a good idea?
   139	
   140	In a word? Risk. If the spec changes in the future, it might be problematic to debug. Read more about it on [sindresorhus's ponyfill](https://github.com/sindresorhus/ponyfill#how-are-ponyfills-better-than-polyfills) page. It's up to you if you're fine with it or not.
   141	
   142	
   143	#### How does cross-fetch work?
   144	
   145	Just like isomorphic-fetch, it is just a proxy. If you're in node, it delivers you the [node-fetch](https://github.com/bitinn/node-fetch/) library, if you're in a browser or React Native, it delivers you the github's [whatwg-fetch](https://github.com/github/fetch/). The same strategy applies whether you're using polyfill or ponyfill.
   146	
   147	
   148	## Who's Using It?
   149	
   150	|[![The New York Times](./docs/images/logo-nytimes.png)](https://www.nytimes.com/)|[![Apollo GraphQL](./docs/images/logo-apollo.png)](https://github.com/apollographql/apollo-client/)|[![Facebook](./docs/images/logo-facebook.png)](https://github.com/facebook/fbjs/)|[![Swagger](./docs/images/logo-swagger.png)](https://swagger.io/)|[![VulcanJS](./docs/images/logo-vulcanjs.png)](http://vulcanjs.org)|[![graphql-request](./docs/images/logo-graphql-request.png)](https://github.com/prisma/graphql-request)|
   151	|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
   152	|The New York Times|Apollo GraphQL|Facebook|Swagger|VulcanJS|graphql-request|
   153	
   154	
   155	## Thanks
   156	
   157	Heavily inspired by the works of [matthew-andrews](https://github.com/matthew-andrews). Kudos to him!
   158	
   159	
   160	## License
   161	
   162	cross-fetch is licensed under the [MIT license](https://github.com/lquixada/cross-fetch/blob/main/LICENSE) © [Leonardo Quixadá](https://twitter.com/lquixada/)
   163	
   164	
   165	## Author
   166	
   167	|[![@lquixada](https://avatars0.githubusercontent.com/u/195494?v=4&s=96)](https://github.com/lquixada)|
   168	|:---:|
   169	|[@lquixada](http://www.github.com/lquixada)|
--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/cross-fetch/index.d.ts
     1	/// <reference lib="dom" />
     2	
     3	declare const _fetch: typeof fetch;
     4	declare const _Request: typeof Request;
     5	declare const _Response: typeof Response;
     6	declare const _Headers: typeof Headers;
     7	
     8	declare module "cross-fetch" {
     9	  export const fetch: typeof _fetch;
    10	  export const Request: typeof _Request;
    11	  export const Response: typeof _Response;
    12	  export const Headers: typeof _Headers;
    13	  export default fetch;
    14	}
