--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/tsyringe/README.md
     1	[![Travis](https://img.shields.io/travis/Microsoft/tsyringe.svg)](https://travis-ci.org/Microsoft/tsyringe/)
     2	[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/tsyringe.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsyringe)
     3	[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/tsyringe.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsyringe)
     4	
     5	# TSyringe
     6	
     7	A lightweight dependency injection container for TypeScript/JavaScript for
     8	constructor injection.
     9	
    10	<!-- TOC depthFrom:1 depthTo:3 -->
    11	
    12	- [TSyringe](#tsyringe)
    13	  - [Installation](#installation)
    14	- [API](#api)
    15	  - [Decorators](#decorators)
    16	    - [injectable()](#injectable)
    17	    - [singleton()](#singleton)
    18	    - [autoInjectable()](#autoinjectable)
    19	    - [inject()](#inject)
    20	    - [injectAll()](#injectall)
    21	    - [injectWithTransform()](#injectWithTransform)
    22	    - [injectAllWithTransform()](#injectAllWithTransform)
    23	    - [scoped()](#scoped)
    24	  - [Container](#container)
    25	    - [Injection Token](#injection-token)
    26	    - [Providers](#providers)
    27	    - [Register](#register)
    28	    - [Registry](#registry)
    29	    - [Resolution](#resolution)
    30	    - [IsRegistered](#isregistered)
    31	    - [Interception](#interception)
    32	    - [Child Containers](#child-containers)
    33	    - [Clearing Instances](#clearing-instances)
    34	- [Circular dependencies](#circular-dependencies)
    35	  - [The `delay` helper function](#the-delay-helper-function)
    36	  - [Interfaces and circular dependencies](#interfaces-and-circular-dependencies)
    37	- [Disposable instances](#disposable-instances)
    38	- [Full examples](#full-examples)
    39	  - [Example without interfaces](#example-without-interfaces)
    40	  - [Example with interfaces](#example-with-interfaces)
    41	  - [Injecting primitive values (Named injection)](#injecting-primitive-values-named-injection)
    42	- [Non goals](#non-goals)
    43	- [Contributing](#contributing)
    44	
    45	<!-- /TOC -->
    46	
    47	## Installation
    48	
    49	Install by `npm`
    50	
    51	```sh
    52	npm install --save tsyringe
    53	```
    54	
    55	**or** install with `yarn` (this project is developed using `yarn`)
    56	
    57	```sh
    58	yarn add tsyringe
    59	```
    60	
    61	Modify your `tsconfig.json` to include the following settings
    62	
    63	```json
    64	{
    65	  "compilerOptions": {
    66	    "experimentalDecorators": true,
    67	    "emitDecoratorMetadata": true
    68	  }
    69	}
    70	```
    71	
    72	Add a polyfill for the Reflect API (examples below use reflect-metadata). You can use:
    73	
    74	- [reflect-metadata](https://www.npmjs.com/package/reflect-metadata)
    75	- [core-js (core-js/es7/reflect)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/core-js)
    76	- [reflection](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@abraham/reflection)
    77	
    78	The Reflect polyfill import should only be added once, and before DI is used:
    79	
    80	```typescript
    81	// main.ts
    82	import "reflect-metadata";
    83	
    84	// Your code here...
    85	```
    86	
    87	### Babel
    88	
    89	If you're using Babel (e.g. using React Native), you will need to configure it to emit TypeScript metadata.
    90	
    91	First get the Babel plugin
    92	
    93	#### Yarn
    94	
    95	```
    96	yarn add --dev babel-plugin-transform-typescript-metadata
    97	```
    98	
    99	#### npm
   100	
   101	```
   102	npm install --save-dev babel-plugin-transform-typescript-metadata
   103	```
   104	
   105	Then add it to your Babel config
   106	
   107	```
   108	plugins: [
   109	            'babel-plugin-transform-typescript-metadata',
   110	            /* ...the rest of your config... */
   111	         ]
   112	```
   113	
   114	# API
   115	
   116	TSyringe performs [Constructor Injection](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dependency_injection#Constructor_injection)
   117	on the constructors of decorated classes.
   118	
   119	## Decorators
   120	
   121	### injectable()
   122	
   123	Class decorator factory that allows the class' dependencies to be injected at
   124	runtime. TSyringe relies on several decorators in order to collect metadata about classes
   125	to be instantiated.
   126	
   127	#### Usage
   128	
   129	```typescript
   130	import {injectable} from "tsyringe";
   131	
   132	@injectable()
   133	class Foo {
   134	  constructor(private database: Database) {}
   135	}
   136	
   137	// some other file
   138	import "reflect-metadata";
   139	import {container} from "tsyringe";
   140	import {Foo} from "./foo";
   141	
   142	const instance = container.resolve(Foo);
   143	```
   144	
   145	### singleton()
   146	
   147	Class decorator factory that registers the class as a singleton within the
   148	global container.
   149	
   150	#### Usage
   151	
   152	```typescript
   153	import {singleton} from "tsyringe";
   154	
   155	@singleton()
   156	class Foo {
   157	  constructor() {}
   158	}
   159	
   160	// some other file
   161	import "reflect-metadata";
   162	import {container} from "tsyringe";
   163	import {Foo} from "./foo";
   164	
   165	const instance = container.resolve(Foo);
   166	```
   167	
   168	### autoInjectable()
   169	
   170	Class decorator factory that replaces the decorated class' constructor with
   171	a parameterless constructor that has dependencies auto-resolved.
   172	
   173	**Note** Resolution is performed using the global container.
   174	
   175	#### Usage
   176	
   177	```typescript
   178	import {autoInjectable} from "tsyringe";
   179	
   180	@autoInjectable()
   181	class Foo {
   182	  constructor(private database?: Database) {}
   183	}
   184	
   185	// some other file
   186	import {Foo} from "./foo";
   187	
   188	const instance = new Foo();
   189	```
   190	
   191	Notice how in order to allow the use of the empty constructor `new Foo()`, we
   192	need to make the parameters optional, e.g. `database?: Database`.
   193	
   194	### inject()
   195	
   196	Parameter decorator factory that allows for interface and other non-class
   197	information to be stored in the constructor's metadata.
   198	
   199	#### Usage
   200	
   201	```typescript
   202	import {injectable, inject} from "tsyringe";
   203	
   204	interface Database {
   205	  // ...
   206	}
   207	
   208	@injectable()
   209	class Foo {
   210	  constructor(@inject("Database") private database?: Database) {}
   211	}
   212	```
   213	
   214	#### Optional
   215	By default, `@inject()` throws an exception if no registration is found. If you want to have `undefined` injected when the registration isn't found, you can pass this options `{ isOptional: true }` as the second parameter:
   216	
   217	```typescript
   218	import {injectable, injectAll} from "tsyringe";
   219	
   220	@injectable()
--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/tsyringe/SECURITY.md
     1	<!-- BEGIN MICROSOFT SECURITY.MD V0.0.7 BLOCK -->
     2	
     3	## Security
     4	
     5	Microsoft takes the security of our software products and services seriously, which includes all source code repositories managed through our GitHub organizations, which include [Microsoft](https://github.com/Microsoft), [Azure](https://github.com/Azure), [DotNet](https://github.com/dotnet), [AspNet](https://github.com/aspnet), [Xamarin](https://github.com/xamarin), and [our GitHub organizations](https://opensource.microsoft.com/).
     6	
     7	If you believe you have found a security vulnerability in any Microsoft-owned repository that meets [Microsoft's definition of a security vulnerability](https://aka.ms/opensource/security/definition), please report it to us as described below.
     8	
     9	## Reporting Security Issues
    10	
    11	**Please do not report security vulnerabilities through public GitHub issues.**
    12	
    13	Instead, please report them to the Microsoft Security Response Center (MSRC) at [https://msrc.microsoft.com/create-report](https://aka.ms/opensource/security/create-report).
    14	
    15	If you prefer to submit without logging in, send email to [secure@microsoft.com](mailto:secure@microsoft.com).  If possible, encrypt your message with our PGP key; please download it from the [Microsoft Security Response Center PGP Key page](https://aka.ms/opensource/security/pgpkey).
    16	
    17	You should receive a response within 24 hours. If for some reason you do not, please follow up via email to ensure we received your original message. Additional information can be found at [microsoft.com/msrc](https://aka.ms/opensource/security/msrc). 
    18	
    19	Please include the requested information listed below (as much as you can provide) to help us better understand the nature and scope of the possible issue:
    20	
    21	  * Type of issue (e.g. buffer overflow, SQL injection, cross-site scripting, etc.)
    22	  * Full paths of source file(s) related to the manifestation of the issue
    23	  * The location of the affected source code (tag/branch/commit or direct URL)
    24	  * Any special configuration required to reproduce the issue
    25	  * Step-by-step instructions to reproduce the issue
    26	  * Proof-of-concept or exploit code (if possible)
    27	  * Impact of the issue, including how an attacker might exploit the issue
    28	
    29	This information will help us triage your report more quickly.
    30	
    31	If you are reporting for a bug bounty, more complete reports can contribute to a higher bounty award. Please visit our [Microsoft Bug Bounty Program](https://aka.ms/opensource/security/bounty) page for more details about our active programs.
    32	
    33	## Preferred Languages
    34	
    35	We prefer all communications to be in English.
    36	
    37	## Policy
    38	
    39	Microsoft follows the principle of [Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure](https://aka.ms/opensource/security/cvd).
    40	
    41	<!-- END MICROSOFT SECURITY.MD BLOCK -->

     1	{
     2	  "name": "tsyringe",
     3	  "version": "4.10.0",
     4	  "description": "Lightweight dependency injection container for JavaScript/TypeScript",
     5	  "main": "dist/cjs/index.js",
     6	  "module": "./dist/esm5/index.js",
     7	  "es2015": "./dist/esm2015/index.js",
     8	  "typings": "./dist/typings/index.d.ts",
     9	  "scripts": {
    10	    "build": "yarn clean && yarn build:cjs && yarn build:es5 && yarn build:es2015 && yarn build:types",
    11	    "build:cjs": "tsc",
    12	    "build:es5": "tsc -p ./typescript/tsconfig.esm5.json",
    13	    "build:es2015": "tsc -p ./typescript/tsconfig.esm2015.json",
    14	    "build:types": "tsc -p ./typescript/tsconfig.types.json",
    15	    "clean": "rimraf ./dist",
    16	    "test": "yarn lint && jest --config test/jest.config.js",
    17	    "test:inspect": "yarn lint && node --inspect-brk ./node_modules/jest/bin/jest.js --runInBand --config test/jest.config.js",
    18	    "test:coverage": "jest --config test/jest.config.js --coverage",
    19	    "lint": "eslint --ext \".js,.jsx,.ts,.tsx\" \"./src\"",
    20	    "lint:fix": "eslint --fix --ext \".js,.jsx,.ts,.tsx\" \"./src\""
    21	  },
    22	  "repository": {
    23	    "type": "git",
    24	    "url": "git+https://github.com/Microsoft/tsyringe.git"
    25	  },
    26	  "keywords": [
    27	    "dependency injection",
    28	    "dependency",
    29	    "injection",
    30	    "ioc",
    31	    "container",
    32	    "javascript",
    33	    "typescript"
    34	  ],
    35	  "engines": {
    36	    "node": ">= 6.0.0"
    37	  },
    38	  "author": "Steven Hobson-Campbell",
    39	  "license": "MIT",
    40	  "bugs": {
    41	    "url": "https://github.com/Microsoft/tsyringe/issues"
    42	  },
    43	  "homepage": "https://github.com/Microsoft/tsyringe#readme",
    44	  "dependencies": {
    45	    "tslib": "^1.9.3"
    46	  },
    47	  "devDependencies": {
    48	    "@types/jest": "^24.0.21",
    49	    "@types/node": "^8.10.16",
    50	    "@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin": "^2.6.0",
    51	    "@typescript-eslint/parser": "^2.6.0",
    52	    "eslint": "^6.6.0",
    53	    "eslint-config-prettier": "^6.5.0",
    54	    "eslint-plugin-prettier": "^3.1.1",
    55	    "husky": "^3.0.0",
    56	    "jest": "^24.7.1",
    57	    "prettier": "1.18.2",
    58	    "reflect-metadata": "^0.1.12",
    59	    "rimraf": "^3.0.0",
    60	    "ts-jest": "^24.0.2",
    61	    "typescript": "^3.1.6"
    62	  }
    63	}

--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/webidl-conversions/README.md
     1	# WebIDL Type Conversions on JavaScript Values
     2	
     3	This package implements, in JavaScript, the algorithms to convert a given JavaScript value according to a given [WebIDL](http://heycam.github.io/webidl/) [type](http://heycam.github.io/webidl/#idl-types).
     4	
     5	The goal is that you should be able to write code like
     6	
     7	```js
     8	const conversions = require("webidl-conversions");
     9	
    10	function doStuff(x, y) {
    11	    x = conversions["boolean"](x);
    12	    y = conversions["unsigned long"](y);
    13	    // actual algorithm code here
    14	}
    15	```
    16	
    17	and your function `doStuff` will behave the same as a WebIDL operation declared as
    18	
    19	```webidl
    20	void doStuff(boolean x, unsigned long y);
    21	```
    22	
    23	## API
    24	
    25	This package's main module's default export is an object with a variety of methods, each corresponding to a different WebIDL type. Each method, when invoked on a JavaScript value, will give back the new JavaScript value that results after passing through the WebIDL conversion rules. (See below for more details on what that means.) Alternately, the method could throw an error, if the WebIDL algorithm is specified to do so: for example `conversions["float"](NaN)` [will throw a `TypeError`](http://heycam.github.io/webidl/#es-float).
    26	
    27	## Status
    28	
    29	All of the numeric types are implemented (float being implemented as double) and some others are as well - check the source for all of them. This list will grow over time in service of the [HTML as Custom Elements](https://github.com/dglazkov/html-as-custom-elements) project, but in the meantime, pull requests welcome!
    30	
    31	I'm not sure yet what the strategy will be for modifiers, e.g. [`[Clamp]`](http://heycam.github.io/webidl/#Clamp). Maybe something like `conversions["unsigned long"](x, { clamp: true })`? We'll see.
    32	
    33	We might also want to extend the API to give better error messages, e.g. "Argument 1 of HTMLMediaElement.fastSeek is not a finite floating-point value" instead of "Argument is not a finite floating-point value." This would require passing in more information to the conversion functions than we currently do.
    34	
    35	## Background
    36	
    37	What's actually going on here, conceptually, is pretty weird. Let's try to explain.
    38	
    39	WebIDL, as part of its madness-inducing design, has its own type system. When people write algorithms in web platform specs, they usually operate on WebIDL values, i.e. instances of WebIDL types. For example, if they were specifying the algorithm for our `doStuff` operation above, they would treat `x` as a WebIDL value of [WebIDL type `boolean`](http://heycam.github.io/webidl/#idl-boolean). Crucially, they would _not_ treat `x` as a JavaScript variable whose value is either the JavaScript `true` or `false`. They're instead working in a different type system altogether, with its own rules.
    40	
    41	Separately from its type system, WebIDL defines a ["binding"](http://heycam.github.io/webidl/#ecmascript-binding) of the type system into JavaScript. This contains rules like: when you pass a JavaScript value to the JavaScript method that manifests a given WebIDL operation, how does that get converted into a WebIDL value? For example, a JavaScript `true` passed in the position of a WebIDL `boolean` argument becomes a WebIDL `true`. But, a JavaScript `true` passed in the position of a [WebIDL `unsigned long`](http://heycam.github.io/webidl/#idl-unsigned-long) becomes a WebIDL `1`. And so on.
    42	
    43	Finally, we have the actual implementation code. This is usually C++, although these days [some smart people are using Rust](https://github.com/servo/servo). The implementation, of course, has its own type system. So when they implement the WebIDL algorithms, they don't actually use WebIDL values, since those aren't "real" outside of specs. Instead, implementations apply the WebIDL binding rules in such a way as to convert incoming JavaScript values into C++ values. For example, if code in the browser called `doStuff(true, true)`, then the implementation code would eventually receive a C++ `bool` containing `true` and a C++ `uint32_t` containing `1`.
    44	
    45	The upside of all this is that implementations can abstract all the conversion logic away, letting WebIDL handle it, and focus on implementing the relevant methods in C++ with values of the correct type already provided. That is payoff of WebIDL, in a nutshell.
    46	
    47	And getting to that payoff is the goal of _this_ project—but for JavaScript implementations, instead of C++ ones. That is, this library is designed to make it easier for JavaScript developers to write functions that behave like a given WebIDL operation. So conceptually, the conversion pipeline, which in its general form is JavaScript values ↦ WebIDL values ↦ implementation-language values, in this case becomes JavaScript values ↦ WebIDL values ↦ JavaScript values. And that intermediate step is where all the logic is performed: a JavaScript `true` becomes a WebIDL `1` in an unsigned long context, which then becomes a JavaScript `1`.
    48	
    49	## Don't Use This
    50	
    51	Seriously, why would you ever use this? You really shouldn't. WebIDL is … not great, and you shouldn't be emulating its semantics. If you're looking for a generic argument-processing library, you should find one with better rules than those from WebIDL. In general, your JavaScript should not be trying to become more like WebIDL; if anything, we should fix WebIDL to make it more like JavaScript.
    52	
    53	The _only_ people who should use this are those trying to create faithful implementations (or polyfills) of web platform interfaces defined in WebIDL.

     1	{

--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/whatwg-url/README.md
     1	﻿# whatwg-url
     2	
     3	whatwg-url is a full implementation of the WHATWG [URL Standard](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/). It can be used standalone, but it also exposes a lot of the internal algorithms that are useful for integrating a URL parser into a project like [jsdom](https://github.com/tmpvar/jsdom).
     4	
     5	## Current Status
     6	
     7	whatwg-url is currently up to date with the URL spec up to commit [a62223](https://github.com/whatwg/url/commit/a622235308342c9adc7fc2fd1659ff059f7d5e2a).
     8	
     9	## API
    10	
    11	### The `URL` Constructor
    12	
    13	The main API is the [`URL`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#url) export, which follows the spec's behavior in all ways (including e.g. `USVString` conversion). Most consumers of this library will want to use this.
    14	
    15	### Low-level URL Standard API
    16	
    17	The following methods are exported for use by places like jsdom that need to implement things like [`HTMLHyperlinkElementUtils`](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#htmlhyperlinkelementutils). They operate on or return an "internal URL" or ["URL record"](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url) type.
    18	
    19	- [URL parser](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-parser): `parseURL(input, { baseURL, encodingOverride })`
    20	- [Basic URL parser](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-basic-url-parser): `basicURLParse(input, { baseURL, encodingOverride, url, stateOverride })`
    21	- [URL serializer](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-serializer): `serializeURL(urlRecord, excludeFragment)`
    22	- [Host serializer](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-host-serializer): `serializeHost(hostFromURLRecord)`
    23	- [Serialize an integer](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#serialize-an-integer): `serializeInteger(number)`
    24	- [Origin](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-origin) [serializer](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/browsers.html#serialization-of-an-origin): `serializeURLOrigin(urlRecord)`
    25	- [Set the username](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#set-the-username): `setTheUsername(urlRecord, usernameString)`
    26	- [Set the password](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#set-the-password): `setThePassword(urlRecord, passwordString)`
    27	- [Cannot have a username/password/port](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#cannot-have-a-username-password-port): `cannotHaveAUsernamePasswordPort(urlRecord)`
    28	
    29	The `stateOverride` parameter is one of the following strings:
    30	
    31	- [`"scheme start"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#scheme-start-state)
    32	- [`"scheme"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#scheme-state)
    33	- [`"no scheme"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#no-scheme-state)
    34	- [`"special relative or authority"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#special-relative-or-authority-state)
    35	- [`"path or authority"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#path-or-authority-state)
    36	- [`"relative"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#relative-state)
    37	- [`"relative slash"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#relative-slash-state)
    38	- [`"special authority slashes"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#special-authority-slashes-state)
    39	- [`"special authority ignore slashes"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#special-authority-ignore-slashes-state)
    40	- [`"authority"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#authority-state)
    41	- [`"host"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#host-state)
    42	- [`"hostname"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#hostname-state)
    43	- [`"port"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#port-state)
    44	- [`"file"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#file-state)
    45	- [`"file slash"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#file-slash-state)
    46	- [`"file host"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#file-host-state)
    47	- [`"path start"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#path-start-state)
    48	- [`"path"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#path-state)
    49	- [`"cannot-be-a-base-URL path"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#cannot-be-a-base-url-path-state)
    50	- [`"query"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#query-state)
    51	- [`"fragment"`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#fragment-state)
    52	
    53	The URL record type has the following API:
    54	
    55	- [`scheme`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-scheme)
    56	- [`username`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-username)
    57	- [`password`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-password)
    58	- [`host`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-host)
    59	- [`port`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-port)
    60	- [`path`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-path) (as an array)
    61	- [`query`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-query)
    62	- [`fragment`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-url-fragment)
    63	- [`cannotBeABaseURL`](https://url.spec.whatwg.org/#url-cannot-be-a-base-url-flag) (as a boolean)
    64	
    65	These properties should be treated with care, as in general changing them will cause the URL record to be in an inconsistent state until the appropriate invocation of `basicURLParse` is used to fix it up. You can see examples of this in the URL Standard, where there are many step sequences like "4. Set context object’s url’s fragment to the empty string. 5. Basic URL parse _input_ with context object’s url as _url_ and fragment state as _state override_." In between those two steps, a URL record is in an unusable state.
    66	
    67	The return value of "failure" in the spec is represented by the string `"failure"`. That is, functions like `parseURL` and `basicURLParse` can return _either_ a URL record _or_ the string `"failure"`.
--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/node_modules/whatwg-url/package.json
     1	{
     2	  "name": "whatwg-url",
     3	  "version": "5.0.0",
     4	  "description": "An implementation of the WHATWG URL Standard's URL API and parsing machinery",
     5	  "main": "lib/public-api.js",
     6	  "files": [
     7	    "lib/"
     8	  ],
     9	  "author": "Sebastian Mayr <github@smayr.name>",
    10	  "license": "MIT",
    11	  "repository": "jsdom/whatwg-url",
    12	  "dependencies": {
    13	    "tr46": "~0.0.3",
    14	    "webidl-conversions": "^3.0.0"
    15	  },
    16	  "devDependencies": {
    17	    "eslint": "^2.6.0",
    18	    "istanbul": "~0.4.3",
    19	    "mocha": "^2.2.4",
    20	    "recast": "~0.10.29",
    21	    "request": "^2.55.0",
    22	    "webidl2js": "^3.0.2"
    23	  },
    24	  "scripts": {
    25	    "build": "node scripts/transform.js && node scripts/convert-idl.js",
    26	    "coverage": "istanbul cover node_modules/mocha/bin/_mocha",
    27	    "lint": "eslint .",
    28	    "prepublish": "npm run build",
    29	    "pretest": "node scripts/get-latest-platform-tests.js && npm run build",
    30	    "test": "mocha"
    31	  }
    32	}

--- tests/Fixtures/Generators/package.json
     1	{
     2	  "name": "turnkey-sdk-csharp-fixture-generators",
     3	  "version": "0.0.0",
     4	  "private": true,
     5	  "type": "module",
     6	  "description": "Node-side fixture generator for the C# SDK equivalence-verification plan (PR-4..PR-7).",
     7	  "engines": {
     8	    "node": ">=20.10.0 <25"
     9	  },
    10	  "scripts": {
    11	    "preflight": "node generate-stamper-vectors.mjs --preflight-only",
    12	    "gen:http": "node generate-http-vectors.mjs",
    13	    "gen:stamper": "node generate-stamper-vectors.mjs",
    14	    "gen:crypto": "node generate-crypto-vectors.mjs",
    15	    "gen:all": "npm run gen:http && npm run gen:stamper && npm run gen:crypto"
    16	  },
    17	  "dependencies": {
    18	    "@turnkey/api-key-stamper": "0.5.0",
    19	    "@turnkey/crypto": "2.8.8",
    20	    "@turnkey/encoding": "0.6.0",
    21	    "@turnkey/http": "3.16.0"
    22	  },
    23	  "overrides": {
    24	    "@noble/curves": "1.3.0",
    25	    "@noble/hashes": "1.3.3"
    26	  }
    27	}

codex
The README fix now explicitly names the deferred plan PRs in both the tier table and a dedicated “NOT enforced” section. I’m doing the second pass now for any mismatch introduced by that wording, plus rechecking the cryptographic fixtures and workflow assumptions rather than relying only on the round-1 transcript.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ApiKeyStamperTests.cs — xunit tests for src/ApiKeyStamper.cs.
     2	//
     3	// Vector sources:
     4	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__fixtures__/api-key.{private,public}
     5	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__tests__/stamp-test.ts
     6	//
     7	// P-256 ECDSA signatures are not deterministic across libraries (noble uses
     8	// RFC 6979; BouncyCastle's HMacDsaKCalculator also uses RFC 6979 with the
     9	// same digest, so for identical input + key + nonce-source the signature
    10	// is deterministic). Tests therefore assert:
    11	//   - the header name == "X-Stamp"
    12	//   - the base64url-decoded JSON has keys ["publicKey", "scheme", "signature"]
    13	//   - publicKey matches the configured key
    14	//   - scheme == "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256"
    15	//   - the signature is a valid DER-encoded ECDSA signature over SHA-256(content)
    16	//     under the configured public key
    17	
    18	using System;
    19	using System.IO;
    20	using System.Text;
    21	using System.Text.Json;
    22	using FluentAssertions;
    23	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1;
    24	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1.X9;
    25	using Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Parameters;
    26	using Org.BouncyCastle.Math;
    27	using Org.BouncyCastle.Security;
    28	using Xunit;
    29	
    30	namespace Turnkey.Tests
    31	{
    32	    public class ApiKeyStamperTests
    33	    {
    34	        // From upstream __fixtures__/api-key.{private,public}
    35	        private const string FixturePrivateKey =
    36	            "487f361ddfd73440e707f4daa6775b376859e8a3c9f29b3bb694a12927c0213c";
    37	        private const string FixturePublicKey =
    38	            "02f739f8c77b32f4d5f13265861febd76e7a9c61a1140d296b8c16302508870316";
    39	
    40	        [Fact]
    41	        public void Constructor_DefersValidation()
    42	        {
    43	            // Upstream `new ApiKeyStamper({apiPublicKey, apiPrivateKey})` only
    44	            // assigns fields; validation happens at sign time. C# mirrors
    45	            // that, including null tolerance.
    46	            Action act = () => new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, "abcd");
    47	            act.Should().NotThrow();
    48	            Action act2 = () => new ApiKeyStamper(null!, null!);
    49	            act2.Should().NotThrow();
    50	        }
    51	
    52	        [Fact]
    53	        public void SignWithApiKey_RejectsBadKeyLength()
    54	        {
    55	            // Validation now occurs in SignWithApiKey -> Crypto.GetPublicKey.
    56	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, "abcd");
    57	            Action act = () => stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
    58	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
    59	               .WithMessage("invalid P-256 private key: expected 32 bytes, got 2");
    60	        }
    61	
    62	        [Fact]
    63	        public void SignWithApiKey_RejectsZeroScalar()
    64	        {
    65	            string zero = new string('0', 64);
    66	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, zero);
    67	            Action act = () => stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
    68	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
    69	               .WithMessage("invalid P-256 private key: scalar must be in [1, n - 1]");
    70	        }
    71	
    72	        /// upstream: codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__tests__/stamp-test.ts:6 "uses provided signature to make stamp"
    73	        [Fact]
    74	        public void Stamp_UpstreamFixture_ProducesValidWireBytes()
    75	        {
    76	            // Matches upstream __tests__/stamp-test.ts:6-37
    77	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
    78	            const string messageToSign = "hello from TKHQ!";
    79	
    80	            var result = stamper.Stamp(messageToSign);
    81	
    82	            result.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
    83	
    84	            // Decode base64url -> JSON -> object.
    85	            string decodedJson = Encoding.DecodeBase64UrlToString(result.StampHeaderValue);
    86	
    87	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(decodedJson);
    88	            var root = doc.RootElement;
    89	
    90	            // Keys must be exactly publicKey, scheme, signature in this order.
    91	            int i = 0;
    92	            string[] expectedKeys = { "publicKey", "scheme", "signature" };
    93	            foreach (var prop in root.EnumerateObject())
    94	            {
    95	                prop.Name.Should().Be(expectedKeys[i++]);
    96	            }
    97	            i.Should().Be(3);
    98	
    99	            root.GetProperty("publicKey").GetString().Should().Be(FixturePublicKey);
   100	            root.GetProperty("scheme").GetString().Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
   101	
   102	            // Verify the DER ECDSA signature against SHA-256(content) under the
   103	            // public key. This is the wire-format gate: if the signature isn't
   104	            // verifiable against the configured P-256 key, the stamp would be
   105	            // rejected by Turnkey's backend.
   106	            string signatureHex = root.GetProperty("signature").GetString()!;
   107	            byte[] signatureBytes = HexToBytes(signatureHex);
   108	            AssertSignatureVerifies(FixturePublicKey, messageToSign, signatureBytes);
   109	        }
   110	
   111	        [Fact]
   112	        public void SignWithApiKey_ProducesDerHexThatVerifies()
   113	        {
   114	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   115	            const string content = "any payload we care to sign";
   116	            string sigHex = stamper.SignWithApiKey(content);
   117	
   118	            // Output is DER hex; even length, starts with 30 (SEQUENCE tag).
   119	            (sigHex.Length % 2).Should().Be(0);
   120	            sigHex.Substring(0, 2).Should().Be("30");
   121	
   122	            AssertSignatureVerifies(FixturePublicKey, content, HexToBytes(sigHex));
   123	        }
   124	
   125	        [Fact]
   126	        public void SignWithApiKey_DeterministicWithRfc6979()
   127	        {
   128	            // RFC 6979 + same key + same content + same SHA-256(content) +
   129	            // same low-S normalization => signature is bit-identical across
   130	            // invocations (HMacDsaKCalculator is deterministic).
   131	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   132	            string a = stamper.SignWithApiKey("the same content");
   133	            string b = stamper.SignWithApiKey("the same content");
   134	            a.Should().Be(b);
   135	        }
   136	
   137	        [Fact]
   138	        public void SignWithApiKey_LowSEnforced()
   139	        {
   140	            // For RFC 6979 + low-S: the s value's high bit (top byte) must be
   141	            // strictly less than (n / 2 + 1). Verify by parsing the DER and
   142	            // checking the s integer is in the lower half of [1, n-1].
   143	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   144	            for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
   145	            {
   146	                string sigHex = stamper.SignWithApiKey("content_" + i);
   147	                var (r, s) = ParseDerEcdsa(HexToBytes(sigHex));
   148	
   149	                var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
   150	                var halfN = curve.N.ShiftRight(1);
   151	                s.CompareTo(halfN).Should().BeLessOrEqualTo(0,
   152	                    "low-S requires s to be in (0, n/2]");
   153	                r.SignValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
   154	                s.SignValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
   155	            }
   156	        }
   157	
   158	        [Fact]
   159	        public void SignWithApiKey_WrongPublicKey_Throws()
   160	        {
   161	            // Mismatched public key (any other valid P-256 compressed key).
   162	            var other = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   163	            Action act = () =>
   164	            {
   165	                var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(other.PublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   166	                stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
   167	            };
   168	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
   169	               .WithMessage("Bad API key. Expected to get public key*");
   170	        }
   171	
   172	        [Fact]
   173	        public void Stamp_HeaderConstantsAndScheme()
   174	        {
   175	            ApiKeyStamper.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
   176	            ApiKeyStamper.SignatureScheme.Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
   177	        }
   178	
   179	        [Fact]
   180	        public void StampHeaderValue_IsAsciiUrlSafe()
   181	        {
   182	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   183	            var result = stamper.Stamp("hello");
   184	            // base64url alphabet: A-Z a-z 0-9 - _
   185	            foreach (char c in result.StampHeaderValue)
   186	            {
   187	                bool ok = (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z')
   188	                          || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z')
   189	                          || (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
   190	                          || c == '-' || c == '_';
   191	                ok.Should().BeTrue($"char '{c}' is not in base64url alphabet");
   192	            }
   193	        }
   194	
   195	        // ============================================================
   196	        // Node-generated byte fixture (PR-6, Tier-2)
   197	        //
   198	        // Loads tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-stamper-node-vectors.json
   199	        // (produced by tests/Fixtures/Generators/generate-stamper-vectors.mjs)
   200	        // and confirms that for each upstream message:
   201	        //
   202	        //   1. C# stamper produces a stamp with the SAME publicKey,
   203	        //      SAME scheme, and SAME JSON key order as upstream.
   204	        //   2. C# stamper produces the SAME RFC 6979 deterministic r
   205	        //      value as upstream (the s value differs because C# enforces
   206	        //      low-S while upstream noble defaults to high-S; the
   207	        //      cryptographic equivalence is s' = n - s).
   208	        //   3. Both the upstream signature AND the C# signature verify
   209	        //      against the public key over SHA-256(message), proving
   210	        //      cryptographic equivalence.
   211	        //
   212	        // This is the "deterministic byte parity" gate for the stamper,
   213	        // expressed at the level where the two implementations agree.
   214	        // ============================================================
   215	
   216	        /// upstream: codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__tests__/signature-test.ts:17 "sign with Turnkey fixture: sign (PureJS)"
   217	        [Fact]
   218	        public void NodeFixture_StamperByteParity_PureJsRfc6979()
   219	        {
   220	            // Resolve the fixture path. With CopyToOutputDirectory=PreserveNewest,
   221	            // the test runner copies the fixture next to the assembly.
   222	            string fixturePath = Path.Combine(
   223	                AppContext.BaseDirectory,
   224	                "Fixtures",
   225	                "api-key-stamper",
   226	                "turnkey-stamper-node-vectors.json");
   227	            File.Exists(fixturePath).Should().BeTrue(
   228	                "fixture must be committed by PR-6 and copied to output");
   229	
   230	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(File.ReadAllText(fixturePath));
   231	            var root = doc.RootElement;
   232	
   233	            // Provenance sanity: must be node-generated with the pinned
   234	            // upstream package + a noble-curves 1.3.0 override.
   235	            var prov = root.GetProperty("_provenance");
   236	            prov.GetProperty("level").GetString().Should().Be("node-generated");
   237	            prov.GetProperty("runtime_override").GetString().Should().Be("purejs");
   238	
   239	            var preflight = root.GetProperty("preflight");
   240	            preflight.GetProperty("deterministic").GetBoolean().Should().BeTrue();
   241	            preflight.GetProperty("schemeOk").GetBoolean().Should().BeTrue();
   242	            preflight.GetProperty("keyOrderOk").GetBoolean().Should().BeTrue();
   243	
   244	            var stamps = root.GetProperty("stamps");
   245	            stamps.GetArrayLength().Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
   246	
   247	            foreach (var entry in stamps.EnumerateArray())
   248	            {
   249	                var input = entry.GetProperty("input");
   250	                string message = input.GetProperty("message").GetString()!;
   251	                string privHex = input.GetProperty("privateKey").GetString()!;
   252	                string pubHex = input.GetProperty("publicKey").GetString()!;
   253	
   254	                var expected = entry.GetProperty("expected");
   255	                string upstreamScheme = expected.GetProperty("decoded")
   256	                    .GetProperty("scheme").GetString()!;
   257	                string upstreamPublicKey = expected.GetProperty("decoded")
   258	                    .GetProperty("publicKey").GetString()!;
   259	                string upstreamSignatureDerHex = expected.GetProperty("decoded")
   260	                    .GetProperty("signature").GetString()!;

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba tests/ProofFixtureProvenanceTests.cs | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ProofFixtureProvenanceTests.cs — PR-11
     2	//
     3	// Asserts the structural integrity and provenance of the upstream proof
     4	// fixture JSON committed at tests/Fixtures/proofs/. The C# proof
     5	// verifier is OUT OF SCOPE per plan Section 12; this test class
     6	// guarantees only:
     7	//
     8	//   1. The fixture JSON parses.
     9	//   2. _provenance carries the expected schema (level, upstream_file,
    10	//      upstream_file_sha256, upstream_package, upstream_package_version).
    11	//   3. The sha256 recorded in _provenance matches the actual sha256 of
    12	//      the cited upstream snapshot file (detects manual edits to either
    13	//      the fixture or the upstream snapshot tree).
    14	//   4. Each appProofs entry has the v1AppProof fields
    15	//      (scheme, publicKey, proofPayload, signature) populated with
    16	//      plausible types (P-256 ephemeral key hex length 264, DER-ish
    17	//      hex strings, SIGNATURE_SCHEME_EPHEMERAL_KEY_P256).
    18	//   5. Each bootProofs entry has the v1BootProof fields populated:
    19	//      ephemeralPublicKeyHex, awsAttestationDocB64, qosManifestB64,
    20	//      qosManifestEnvelopeB64, deploymentLabel, enclaveApp, owner,
    21	//      createdAt {seconds, nanos}.
    22	
    23	using System;
    24	using System.IO;
    25	using System.Linq;
    26	using System.Security.Cryptography;
    27	using System.Text.Json;
    28	using FluentAssertions;
    29	using Xunit;
    30	
    31	namespace Turnkey.Tests
    32	{
    33	    public class ProofFixtureProvenanceTests
    34	    {
    35	        private static readonly string FixturePath = Path.Combine(
    36	            AppContext.BaseDirectory,
    37	            "Fixtures",
    38	            "proofs",
    39	            "upstream-proof-tests-fixture.json");
    40	
    41	        // The fixture is shipped under tests/Fixtures/proofs/ at repo root.
    42	        // AppContext.BaseDirectory points at .../tests/bin/Release/net8.0/.
    43	        // The upstream snapshot it cites lives outside the test bin dir;
    44	        // we resolve the snapshot path by walking up to the repo root.
    45	        private static string ResolveRepoRoot()
    46	        {
    47	            // tests/bin/Release/net8.0/  -> walk up to ".claude/worktrees/<x>"
    48	            // or the actual repo root (containing turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln).
    49	            string dir = AppContext.BaseDirectory;
    50	            for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
    51	            {
    52	                if (File.Exists(Path.Combine(dir, "turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln")))
    53	                {
    54	                    return dir;
    55	                }
    56	                dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(dir)!;
    57	            }
    58	            throw new InvalidOperationException(
    59	                "could not locate repo root from " + AppContext.BaseDirectory);
    60	        }
    61	
    62	        [Fact]
    63	        public void FixtureFile_Exists_AndParsesAsJson()
    64	        {
    65	            File.Exists(FixturePath).Should().BeTrue(
    66	                $"fixture must be committed at {FixturePath}");
    67	
    68	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(File.ReadAllText(FixturePath));
    69	            doc.RootElement.ValueKind.Should().Be(JsonValueKind.Object);
    70	        }
    71	
    72	        [Fact]
    73	        public void Provenance_SchemaIsComplete()
    74	        {
    75	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(File.ReadAllText(FixturePath));
    76	            var prov = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("_provenance");
    77	
    78	            prov.GetProperty("level").GetString().Should().Be("upstream-test-vectors");
    79	            prov.GetProperty("upstream_file").GetString().Should().StartWith(
    80	                "codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-");
    81	            prov.GetProperty("upstream_file_sha256").GetString().Should().MatchRegex("^[0-9a-f]{64}$");
    82	            prov.GetProperty("upstream_package").GetString().Should().Be("@turnkey/crypto");
    83	            prov.GetProperty("upstream_package_version").GetString().Should().NotBeNullOrWhiteSpace();
    84	        }
    85	
    86	        [Fact]
    87	        public void Provenance_UpstreamFileSha256_MatchesActualSnapshotBytes()
    88	        {
    89	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(File.ReadAllText(FixturePath));
    90	            var prov = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("_provenance");
    91	            string upstreamRel = prov.GetProperty("upstream_file").GetString()!;
    92	            string recordedSha = prov.GetProperty("upstream_file_sha256").GetString()!;
    93	
    94	            string repoRoot = ResolveRepoRoot();
    95	            string upstreamAbs = Path.Combine(repoRoot, upstreamRel);
    96	
    97	            File.Exists(upstreamAbs).Should().BeTrue(
    98	                $"upstream snapshot file cited in provenance must exist: {upstreamAbs}");
    99	
   100	            byte[] bytes = File.ReadAllBytes(upstreamAbs);
   101	            string actualSha = Convert.ToHexString(SHA256.HashData(bytes)).ToLowerInvariant();
   102	
   103	            actualSha.Should().Be(
   104	                recordedSha,
   105	                "fixture provenance sha256 must match the actual bytes of the cited upstream snapshot file. " +
   106	                "If this fails, either the fixture is stale or the upstream snapshot was edited locally.");
   107	        }
   108	
   109	        [Fact]
   110	        public void AppProofs_StructuralSchema_IsCorrect()
   111	        {
   112	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(File.ReadAllText(FixturePath));
   113	            var arr = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("appProofs");
   114	            arr.GetArrayLength().Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
   115	
   116	            foreach (var entry in arr.EnumerateArray())
   117	            {
   118	                entry.GetProperty("scheme").GetString().Should().Be(
   119	                    "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_EPHEMERAL_KEY_P256");
   120	
   121	                string publicKey = entry.GetProperty("publicKey").GetString()!;
   122	                publicKey.Should().MatchRegex("^[0-9a-fA-F]+$");
   123	                // The upstream ephemeralPublicKey form is 2 P-256 uncompressed
   124	                // points concatenated (65 + 65 = 130 bytes => 260 hex chars,
   125	                // or with prefix 04 already present => same).
   126	                publicKey.Length.Should().BeInRange(130, 264,
   127	                    "expected P-256 uncompressed-pair hex (130 chars) or longer");
   128	
   129	                string proofPayload = entry.GetProperty("proofPayload").GetString()!;
   130	                proofPayload.Should().StartWith("{").And.EndWith("}");
   131	                // Parse the nested JSON.
   132	                using var payloadDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(proofPayload);
   133	                payloadDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("type").GetString()
   134	                    .Should().Be("APP_PROOF_TYPE_ADDRESS_DERIVATION");
   135	
   136	                string signature = entry.GetProperty("signature").GetString()!;
   137	                signature.Should().MatchRegex("^[0-9a-fA-F]+$");
   138	                signature.Length.Should().BeInRange(64, 256,
   139	                    "P-256 signature in compact-bytes hex (64 chars min) or DER hex");
   140	            }
   141	        }
   142	
   143	        [Fact]
   144	        public void BootProofs_StructuralSchema_IsCorrect()
   145	        {
   146	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(File.ReadAllText(FixturePath));
   147	            var arr = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("bootProofs");
   148	            arr.GetArrayLength().Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
   149	
   150	            foreach (var entry in arr.EnumerateArray())
   151	            {
   152	                string ephHex = entry.GetProperty("ephemeralPublicKeyHex").GetString()!;
   153	                ephHex.Should().MatchRegex("^[0-9a-fA-F]+$");
   154	
   155	                // AWS attestation + QOS manifests are base64.
   156	                string awsB64 = entry.GetProperty("awsAttestationDocB64").GetString()!;
   157	                Action act = () => Convert.FromBase64String(awsB64);
   158	                act.Should().NotThrow(
   159	                    "awsAttestationDocB64 must be valid base64");
   160	
   161	                string qmB64 = entry.GetProperty("qosManifestB64").GetString()!;
   162	                Action act2 = () => Convert.FromBase64String(qmB64);
   163	                act2.Should().NotThrow("qosManifestB64 must be valid base64");
   164	
   165	                string qmEnvB64 = entry.GetProperty("qosManifestEnvelopeB64").GetString()!;
   166	                Action act3 = () => Convert.FromBase64String(qmEnvB64);
   167	                act3.Should().NotThrow("qosManifestEnvelopeB64 must be valid base64");
   168	
   169	                entry.GetProperty("deploymentLabel").GetString()
   170	                    .Should().NotBeNullOrWhiteSpace();
   171	                entry.GetProperty("enclaveApp").GetString()
   172	                    .Should().Be("signer");
   173	                entry.GetProperty("owner").GetString()
   174	                    .Should().Be("tkhq");
   175	
   176	                var createdAt = entry.GetProperty("createdAt");
   177	                createdAt.GetProperty("seconds").GetString()
   178	                    .Should().MatchRegex("^[0-9]+$");
   179	                createdAt.GetProperty("nanos").GetString()
   180	                    .Should().MatchRegex("^[0-9]+$");
   181	            }
   182	        }
   183	
   184	        [Fact]
   185	        public void AppProofs_And_BootProofs_HaveMatchingPublicKeys()
   186	        {
   187	            // The upstream test pairs (appProof_i, bootProof_i) so that
   188	            // appProof.publicKey == bootProof.ephemeralPublicKeyHex.
   189	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(File.ReadAllText(FixturePath));
   190	            var apps = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("appProofs").EnumerateArray().ToList();
   191	            var boots = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("bootProofs").EnumerateArray().ToList();
   192	
   193	            apps.Count.Should().Be(boots.Count,
   194	                "appProofs and bootProofs arrays must be paired (same length)");
   195	
   196	            for (int i = 0; i < apps.Count; i++)
   197	            {
   198	                apps[i].GetProperty("publicKey").GetString()
   199	                    .Should().Be(
   200	                        boots[i].GetProperty("ephemeralPublicKeyHex").GetString(),
   201	                        $"appProof[{i}].publicKey must equal bootProof[{i}].ephemeralPublicKeyHex (upstream pairing rule)");
   202	            }
   203	        }
   204	    }
   205	}

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs | sed -n '247,430p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4
 succeeded in 0ms:
   247	            foreach (var entry in stamps.EnumerateArray())
   248	            {
   249	                var input = entry.GetProperty("input");
   250	                string message = input.GetProperty("message").GetString()!;
   251	                string privHex = input.GetProperty("privateKey").GetString()!;
   252	                string pubHex = input.GetProperty("publicKey").GetString()!;
   253	
   254	                var expected = entry.GetProperty("expected");
   255	                string upstreamScheme = expected.GetProperty("decoded")
   256	                    .GetProperty("scheme").GetString()!;
   257	                string upstreamPublicKey = expected.GetProperty("decoded")
   258	                    .GetProperty("publicKey").GetString()!;
   259	                string upstreamSignatureDerHex = expected.GetProperty("decoded")
   260	                    .GetProperty("signature").GetString()!;
   261	
   262	                var derived = expected.GetProperty("derived");
   263	                string expectedRHex = derived.GetProperty("r_hex").GetString()!;
   264	                string expectedLowSHex =
   265	                    derived.GetProperty("low_s_equivalent_s_hex").GetString()!;
   266	
   267	                // C# stamper.
   268	                var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(pubHex, privHex);
   269	                var stamp = stamper.Stamp(message);
   270	
   271	                stamp.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
   272	                string decodedJson = Encoding.DecodeBase64UrlToString(stamp.StampHeaderValue);
   273	                using var stampDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(decodedJson);
   274	                var stampRoot = stampDoc.RootElement;
   275	
   276	                // JSON key order: ["publicKey", "scheme", "signature"]
   277	                var propNames = new System.Collections.Generic.List<string>();
   278	                foreach (var p in stampRoot.EnumerateObject()) propNames.Add(p.Name);
   279	                propNames.Should().Equal("publicKey", "scheme", "signature");
   280	
   281	                stampRoot.GetProperty("publicKey").GetString().Should().Be(pubHex);
   282	                stampRoot.GetProperty("scheme").GetString().Should().Be(upstreamScheme);
   283	
   284	                string csSigHex = stampRoot.GetProperty("signature").GetString()!;
   285	                var (csR, csS) = ParseDerEcdsa(HexToBytes(csSigHex));
   286	                csR.ToString(16).TrimStart('0').Should().BeEquivalentTo(
   287	                    expectedRHex.TrimStart('0'),
   288	                    "C# RFC 6979 must derive the same k => same r as upstream");
   289	                csS.ToString(16).TrimStart('0').Should().BeEquivalentTo(
   290	                    expectedLowSHex.TrimStart('0'),
   291	                    "C# must emit the low-S form of upstream's high-S signature");
   292	
   293	                // Upstream's high-S sig and C# low-S sig both verify against
   294	                // the same public key over the same SHA-256(message).
   295	                AssertSignatureVerifies(pubHex, message, HexToBytes(upstreamSignatureDerHex));
   296	                AssertSignatureVerifies(pubHex, message, HexToBytes(csSigHex));
   297	
   298	                // The publicKey in the upstream and C# stamps must match
   299	                // exactly (independent of the s-form difference).
   300	                upstreamPublicKey.Should().Be(pubHex);
   301	            }
   302	        }
   303	
   304	        // ============================================================
   305	        // Helpers
   306	        // ============================================================
   307	
   308	        private static byte[] HexToBytes(string hex) => Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(hex);
   309	
   310	        private static void AssertSignatureVerifies(string compressedPubHex, string content, byte[] derSignature)
   311	        {
   312	            byte[] payloadBytes = System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(content);
   313	
   314	            var digest = DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256");
   315	            digest.BlockUpdate(payloadBytes, 0, payloadBytes.Length);
   316	            var hash = new byte[digest.GetDigestSize()];
   317	            digest.DoFinal(hash, 0);
   318	
   319	            var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
   320	            var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
   321	
   322	            byte[] compressedPub = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(compressedPubHex);
   323	            var point = curve.Curve.DecodePoint(compressedPub);
   324	            var publicKey = new ECPublicKeyParameters(point, domainParams);
   325	
   326	            var signer = SignerUtilities.GetSigner("NONEwithECDSA");
   327	            signer.Init(false, publicKey);
   328	            signer.BlockUpdate(hash, 0, hash.Length);
   329	            signer.VerifySignature(derSignature).Should().BeTrue();
   330	        }
   331	
   332	        private static (BigInteger R, BigInteger S) ParseDerEcdsa(byte[] der)
   333	        {
   334	            using var ms = new MemoryStream(der);
   335	            using var reader = new Asn1InputStream(ms);
   336	            var seq = (DerSequence)reader.ReadObject();
   337	            var r = ((DerInteger)seq[0]).Value;
   338	            var s = ((DerInteger)seq[1]).Value;
   339	            return (r, s);
   340	        }
   341	    }
   342	}

            /// <summary>SEC1 uncompressed P-256 public key length, bytes (0x04 + X + Y).</summary>
            public const int UNCOMPRESSED_PUB_KEY_LENGTH_BYTES = 65;

            /// <summary>Production signer used by Turnkey to sign export/import bundles.</summary>
            public const string PRODUCTION_SIGNER_SIGN_PUBLIC_KEY =
                "04cf288fe433cc4e1aa0ce1632feac4ea26bf2f5a09dcfe5a42c398e06898710330f0572882f4dbdf0f5304b8fc8703acd69adca9a4bbf7f5d00d20a5e364b2569";

            /// <summary>Production notarizer used by Turnkey to sign session JWTs.</summary>
            public const string PRODUCTION_NOTARIZER_SIGN_PUBLIC_KEY =
                "04d498aa87ac3bf982ac2b5dd9604d0074905cfbda5d62727c5a237b895e6749205e9f7cd566909c4387f6ca25c308445c60884b788560b785f4a96ac33702a469";
        }

        #endregion

        #region Curve enum (turnkey.ts type)

  "qos_encryption_hmac_message",
); // used for encrypting messages to quorum keys matched whats found here: https://github.com/tkhq/qos/blob/ae01904c756107f850aea42000137ef124df3fe4/src/qos_p256/src/encrypt.rs#L22
export const PRODUCTION_SIGNER_SIGN_PUBLIC_KEY =
  "04cf288fe433cc4e1aa0ce1632feac4ea26bf2f5a09dcfe5a42c398e06898710330f0572882f4dbdf0f5304b8fc8703acd69adca9a4bbf7f5d00d20a5e364b2569";

export const PRODUCTION_NOTARIZER_SIGN_PUBLIC_KEY =

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba tests/Fixtures/README.md | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Test fixtures
     2	
     3	Each subdirectory groups fixtures for one production file, plus a
     4	provenance note explaining where the bytes came from.
     5	
     6	## Provenance levels
     7	
     8	- **upstream-test-vectors** — directly copied from the upstream
     9	  `__tests__/` directory inside `codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`.
    10	- **rfc** — standardized test vectors (RFC 5869, RFC 9180, etc.).
    11	- **nist** — NIST CAVP P-256 vectors.
    12	- **turnkey-sample** — sample bundles published by Turnkey in their
    13	  developer docs (de-identified).
    14	- **node-generated** — bytes generated by running the pinned upstream Node
    15	  package inside `tests/Fixtures/Generators/`. The generator script,
    16	  Node version, and output hash are recorded so reviewers can re-run.
    17	
    18	## Layout
    19	
    20	```
    21	Fixtures/
    22	├── encoding/
    23	│   └── turnkey-encoding-vectors.json       — upstream-test-vectors
    24	├── crypto/                                  — (added with Crypto.cs port)
    25	├── api-key-stamper/                         — (added with ApiKeyStamper.cs port)
    26	└── http/                                    — (added with Http.cs port)
    27	```
    28	
    29	## Re-generating
    30	
    31	Fixtures sourced from upstream test files are stable as long as
    32	[`codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`](../../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/)
    33	is not bumped. If a pin is bumped, re-extract the upstream `__tests__/`
    34	into the matching fixture file and update its sha256 in
    35	`tarball-checksums.txt` next to the new tarball.

README.md:84:packages is enforced by four cooperating mechanisms ([plan](./plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md)
README.md:89:| 1 | Hand-transcribed upstream `__tests__/` values + cross-language hex constants borrowed from `tkhq/sdk` / `swift-sdk` / `dart-sdk` | `tests/EncodingTests.cs`, `tests/CryptoTests.cs`, `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs`, `tests/Fixtures/{encoding,crypto,api-key-stamper}/*.json`, `tests/ProofFixtureProvenanceTests.cs` | **Fully enforced**; the `coverage-map.sh --check` CI step is the gate. |

HTTP byte vectors (plan PR-5), HPKE round-trip + structural fixtures (plan PR-7), and the SDK HPKE ↔ RFC 9180 byte-equality evaluation (plan PR-7a) are NOT enforced by code yet. They are tracked as deferred follow-up PRs in [`plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md`](./plans/PLAN-EQUIVALENCE-VERIFICATION.md) Section 7. |
README.md:91:| 3 | RFC / NIST / SEC2 / BIP standard vectors for each cryptographic primitive (HKDF RFC 5869, etc.) — primitive-level independent oracle of correctness | `tests/CryptoTests.cs` HKDF tests (RFC 5869 A.1–A.3 only) | **Partial.** Only HKDF is committed. ECDSA P-256 RFC 6979 vectors, AES-GCM-256 NIST CAVP, SHA-256 NIST SHS, HMAC-SHA256 RFC 4231, Ed25519 RFC 8032, secp256k1 SEC2, and Base58Check Bitcoin BIP test vectors are NOT enforced by code yet (plan PR-8 / PR-9 / PR-9b). |
README.md:116:### Deferred enforcement (NOT enforced by this version of the SDK)
README.md:119:of the SDK does NOT yet enforce. Until the corresponding PR lands they

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba codex-crypto-reviews/turnkey-source-pins.md | sed -n '1,180p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Turnkey upstream source pins
     2	
     3	The C# port targets the **peak-pinned** Turnkey npm versions, NOT the Unity
     4	port's newer versions. The npm tarball contents (extracted under
     5	[`upstream-snapshots/`](./upstream-snapshots/)) are the authoritative source.
     6	GitHub SHAs are secondary metadata.
     7	
     8	## Pin table
     9	
    10	| src C# file                | Upstream snapshot path                  | npm package                | Version | Tarball sha256 |
    11	|---|---|---|---|---|
    12	| src/Encoding.cs            | turnkey-encoding-0.6.0                  | @turnkey/encoding          | 0.6.0   | `2cf9e6ee1f47ac7e3cc3e644cdb0e3e112c906a6ea1af737777f4658b73fb7bc` |
    13	| src/CryptoConstants.cs     | turnkey-crypto-2.8.8                    | @turnkey/crypto            | 2.8.8   | `75115706fccc29c664f9f918d9a0c2c1798eb03f261cb5b0c186c75663cf79d3` |
    14	| src/Crypto.cs              | turnkey-crypto-2.8.8                    | @turnkey/crypto            | 2.8.8   | `75115706fccc29c664f9f918d9a0c2c1798eb03f261cb5b0c186c75663cf79d3` |
    15	| src/ApiKeyStamper.cs       | turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0           | @turnkey/api-key-stamper   | 0.5.0   | `962a2d22c7c40240f05be98533769b37ab7dad7dbb5abec762c41007233d02bd` |
    16	| src/Http.cs                | turnkey-http-3.16.0                     | @turnkey/http              | 3.16.0  | `d849f2156633f63062c52067785df1ce33eac0659044df932862c5d6de9dbdaf` |
    17	| src/TurnkeyJsonContext.cs  | turnkey-crypto-2.8.8                    | (no single upstream; covers DTOs from crypto + api-key-stamper + http) | n/a | n/a |
    18	
    19	Each upstream snapshot directory contains:
    20	
    21	- `package/` — verbatim npm tarball extract (the authoritative wire-format
    22	  source: `dist/*.js` + `dist/*.d.ts` + `package.json` + LICENSE / README).
    23	- `ts-source/` — the original TypeScript source from `github.com/tkhq/sdk` at
    24	  the matching git tag SHA (committed for human readability; the npm tarball
    25	  remains the wire-format source of truth if the two ever disagree).
    26	
    27	## Why these versions
    28	
    29	These are exactly what the peak monorepo pulls. See
    30	[`peak-lockfile-evidence.md`](./peak-lockfile-evidence.md) for the
    31	`pnpm-lock.yaml` lines.
    32	
    33	## GitHub commit SHAs (secondary)
    34	
    35	`gitHead` is **NOT present** in the published tarballs for these versions
    36	(`grep gitHead package/package.json` returns nothing for any of the four).
    37	The GitHub commit SHAs below are resolved from the git tag of the form
    38	`@turnkey/<pkg>@<version>` via `gh api /repos/tkhq/sdk/git/ref/tags/...`.
    39	These are recorded for cross-reference only. The npm tarball sha256 above
    40	is the controlling wire-format source.
    41	
    42	| npm package                | Version | Git tag SHA                                  | Reference URL |
    43	|---|---|---|---|
    44	| @turnkey/encoding          | 0.6.0   | `60a997f4c52ac5f98bdd285af934f02699b88bff`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/60a997f4c52ac5f98bdd285af934f02699b88bff/packages/encoding |
    45	| @turnkey/crypto            | 2.8.8   | `b35dc642bd7c1728f97acd43d4cba66976b65084`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/b35dc642bd7c1728f97acd43d4cba66976b65084/packages/crypto |
    46	| @turnkey/api-key-stamper   | 0.5.0   | `b711befbb88ec522452dbdac68f0e98762be10dd`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/b711befbb88ec522452dbdac68f0e98762be10dd/packages/api-key-stamper |
    47	| @turnkey/http              | 3.16.0  | `8def9ba521233137437ac7294693a4ae0a0d14da`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/8def9ba521233137437ac7294693a4ae0a0d14da/packages/http |
    48	
    49	## Re-pinning procedure
    50	
    51	When bumping any pin:
    52	
    53	1. Update the table above (npm version, refresh tarball sha256, gitHead).
    54	2. Update `Directory.Packages.props` or any version comment.
    55	3. Re-run `npm pack` and refresh the extracted directories under
    56	   `upstream-snapshots/`.
    57	4. Re-run **3 Codex review rounds per affected file** via
    58	   [`codex-crypto-review.sh`](./codex-crypto-review.sh).
    59	5. Update `CHANGELOG.md`.

codex
**A. Diff Overview**

Reviewed `origin/main..HEAD` with HEAD `82a54f3`. Commits present:

`a0fd24d` plan v3, `ef3ed59` peak usage evidence, `1a1529e` coverage matrix, `76de11e` export-bundle tests + coverage gate, `2519b2c` generator scaffold, `2a8df82` stamper vectors, `c959e2a` upstream-drift workflow, `ccce1ed` proof fixtures, `d7871bc` README/NOTICE, `82a54f3` D1 README fix.

Implemented: PR-1, PR-1b, PR-2, PR-3, PR-4, PR-6, PR-11, PR-12, PR-14. Deferred and now explicitly documented: PR-5, PR-7, PR-7a, PR-8 except HKDF, PR-9, PR-9b, PR-10, PR-13.

**B. Cryptographic Correctness**

No findings.

PR-6 high-S/low-S math checks out: for preflight and all three stamper vectors, `s_low = n - s_high`, all upstream `s` values are high-S, and all recorded low-S values are in the low half. The C# test verifies both upstream and C# signatures against the same public key and checks `r` equality.

PR-3 production signer key matches byte-for-byte between `Crypto.Constants.PRODUCTION_SIGNER_SIGN_PUBLIC_KEY`, upstream `constants.ts`, and the embedded export bundle in [CryptoTests.cs](/Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4/tests/CryptoTests.cs:633).

PR-11 proof schema matches the upstream JSON: `SIGNATURE_SCHEME_EPHEMERAL_KEY_P256`, `enclaveApp=signer`, `owner=tkhq`, and paired `publicKey == ephemeralPublicKeyHex`.

**C. CI / Operational Risk**

No findings.

`coverage-map.sh --check` depends on normal Ubuntu runner tools: `bash`, `find`, `sort`, `awk`, `mktemp`, `rm`; no `gh` path is used there.

`upstream-drift.yml` uses `gh api`, `gh label create`, `gh issue list/edit/comment/create/close`; declared `contents: read` + `issues: write` is sufficient for this repo’s private issue operations and API reads at the pinned upstream refs.

Generator lockfile has Node `>=20.10.0 <25`, pins `@noble/curves@1.3.0` / `@noble/hashes@1.3.3`, and I found no `os`, `cpu`, or optional platform-specific lockfile entries.

**D. Plan Consistency**

No findings. D1 is resolved.

[README.md](/Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4/README.md:87) now adds `Coverage today`, and [README.md](/Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp/.claude/worktrees/laughing-hertz-048cd4/README.md:116) adds a dedicated “NOT enforced” section. It unambiguously says PR-5, PR-7, PR-7a, PR-8 except HKDF, PR-9, PR-9b, PR-10, and PR-13 are not code-enforced today.

No new overpromise/underpromise mismatch: Tier-2 and Tier-3 are marked partial, Tier-4 opt-in, and PR-10’s deferred note states that some HPKE paths still use generated keys. `coverage-map.tsv` has `REVIEWED: 22`, `N/A: 28`, `MISSING: 0`.

**E. C# / Test Code Quality**

No findings.

The stamper fixture test’s JSON property access fails deterministically on malformed fixtures via `JsonDocument`/`GetProperty`/assertions, not via hidden null dereferences. `ResolveRepoRoot` has enough depth for normal local and GitHub Actions test output paths. I did not find blocking magic-string issues.

**F. Documentation / Attribution**

No findings.

NOTICE points Apache-2.0 borrowings to `tkhq/sdk`, the pinned source table, and the proof fixture source. `turnkey-source-pins.md` records git tag SHAs and repo URLs. README is now explicit that “byte-equal” applies only to committed deterministic paths, with HPKE/RFC9180 and HTTP byte fixtures deferred.

**G. Unresolved Findings**

None.

Round 2 result: PASS — APPROVE.
